what: The one rule: key statistic by the ARD’s context
title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: pivot_across(statistic = list(...)) matches list names ARD’s context column verbatim. value depends function built ARD — use wrong key rows dropped silently. Always check unique(ard$context) first. single string, statistic = list(default = ...), applies one format every context.

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: cdisc_saf_demo_ard stacked continuous categorical block, carries continuous categorical contexts: decimals = c(p = 0) gives integer percent pharma <1 / >99 thresholds; n = 0 cells collapse bare "0" automatically. widened frame now ready tabular() — see Structure.
code: # how it was built (data-raw): cdisc_saf_demo_ard <- cards::ard_stack(   adsl,   .by = TRT01A,   cards::ard_continuous(     variables = c(AGE, BMI),     statistic = ~ cards::continuous_summary_fns(c(       "N",       "mean",       "sd",       "median",       "min",       "max"     ))   ),   cards::ard_categorical(variables = c(AGEGR1, SEX, RACE)) ) data(cdisc_saf_demo_ard, package = "tabular") unique(cdisc_saf_demo_ard$context) #> [1] "continuous"  "categorical" "tabulate"  wide <- pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous = c(       N = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",       Median = "{median}",       "Min, Max" = "{min}, {max}"     ),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   # CDISC precision: SD carries one more decimal than the mean. AGE/BMI are   # whole numbers here (raw precision d = 0), so mean = d + 1 = 1, sd = d + 2 = 2,   # median = d + 1 = 1, and min/max keep the raw precision (0).   decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, median = 1, min = 0, max = 0, p = 0) ) head(wide, 8) #>   variable stat_label      Placebo Xanomeline High Dose Xanomeline Low Dose #> 1      AGE          N           86                   72                  96 #> 2      AGE  Mean (SD)  75.2 (8.59)          73.8 (7.94)         76.0 (8.11) #> 3      AGE     Median         76.0                 75.5                78.0 #> 4      AGE   Min, Max       52, 89               56, 88              51, 88 #> 5   WEIGHT          N           86                   72                  95 #> 6   WEIGHT  Mean (SD) 62.8 (12.77)         69.5 (14.35)        68.0 (14.50) #> 7   WEIGHT     Median         60.6                 69.0                66.7 #> 8   WEIGHT   Min, Max       34, 86              44, 108             42, 106 #>          Total #> 1          254 #> 2  75.1 (8.25) #> 3         77.0 #> 4       51, 89 #> 5          253 #> 6 66.6 (14.13) #> 7         66.7 #> 8      34, 108

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: cardx CI functions emit contexts (proportion_ci, continuous_ci) estimate / conf.low / conf.high stats. pivot_across() consumes frame follows cards long-format, can feed cardx ARD build rows hand — useful statistic computed outside cards:
code: # from cardx: orr_ard <- cardx::ard_categorical_ci(   resp,   by = "TRT01A",   variables = "RESP",   method = "clopper-pearson",   conf.level = 0.95 ) # equivalent hand-built ARD (one row group, proportion_ci context). # tribble() lays the long ARD out as a literal table: one row per # (arm, stat), with the estimate and its CI bounds visible inline. orr_ard <- tibble::tribble(   ~group1,  ~group1_level,          ~variable, ~variable_level, ~context,        ~stat_name,  ~stat,   "TRT01A", "Placebo",              "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "estimate",  0.62,   "TRT01A", "Placebo",              "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.low",  0.50,   "TRT01A", "Placebo",              "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.high", 0.73,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline Low Dose",  "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "estimate",  0.55,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline Low Dose",  "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.low",  0.42,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline Low Dose",  "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.high", 0.67,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline High Dose", "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "estimate",  0.48,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline High Dose", "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.low",  0.36,   "TRT01A", "Xanomeline High Dose", "RESP",    "Responders",    "proportion_ci", "conf.high", 0.60, )  pivot_across(   orr_ard,   statistic = list(proportion_ci = "{estimate} ({conf.low}, {conf.high})"),   decimals = c(estimate = 3, conf.low = 3, conf.high = 3) ) #>   variable stat_label              Placebo  Xanomeline Low Dose #> 1     RESP Responders 0.620 (0.500, 0.730) 0.550 (0.420, 0.670) #>   Xanomeline High Dose #> 1 0.480 (0.360, 0.600)

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: hierarchical ARD widens soc / label / row_type triple (single variable), ready indented SOC ▸ PT layout: (Turning soc/label/indent_level indented stub Structure.)
code: data(cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard, package = "tabular") ae <- pivot_across(cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard, statistic = "{n} ({p}%)") ae$indent_level <- as.integer(ae$row_type == "pt") # depth for col_spec(indent = "indent_level") head(ae, 6) #>                                      soc                                  label #> 1                                Overall                                Overall #> 2 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS #> 3 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                               PRURITUS #> 4 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                               ERYTHEMA #> 5 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                                   RASH #> 6 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                          HYPERHIDROSIS #>   row_type  Placebo Xanomeline High Dose Xanomeline Low Dose indent_level #> 1  overall 52 (60%)             66 (92%)            81 (84%)            0 #> 2      soc 19 (22%)             35 (49%)            36 (38%)            0 #> 3       pt   8 (9%)             25 (35%)            21 (22%)            1 #> 4       pt   8 (9%)             14 (19%)            14 (15%)            1 #> 5       pt   5 (6%)              8 (11%)            13 (14%)            1 #> 6       pt   2 (2%)              8 (11%)              4 (4%)            1

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: ard_stack(.= c(ARM, SEX)) carries second grouping variable. Name row_group = pivot_across() widens leading row column (rather mis-reading SOC/PT hierarchy). cards long shape stack produces (RACE ARM within SEX), built hand example runs without cards: 2-variable .present must say variable arm column (column =) row dimension (row_group =); SEX column composes subgroup() col_spec(usage = "group") downstream. cards encodes crossing factor real hierarchy identically, declaration disambiguates — leave row_group unset genuine SOC/PT hierarchy.
code: # RACE by ARM within SEX, laid out as a literal tribble: one row per # (sex, race, arm, stat). Stats are placeholders (n = 10, p = 0.25). two_by_ard <- tibble::tribble(   ~group1, ~group1_level, ~group2, ~group2_level, ~variable, ~variable_level, ~context,      ~stat_name, ~stat,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "F",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "WHITE",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Placebo",     "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "p",        0.25,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "n",        10,   "ARM",   "Drug",        "SEX",   "M",           "RACE",    "BLACK",         "categorical", "p",        0.25, )  pivot_across(   two_by_ard,   column = "ARM",   row_group = "SEX",   statistic = list(categorical = "{n} ({p}%)") ) #>   SEX variable stat_label  Placebo     Drug #> 1   F     RACE      WHITE 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 2   F     RACE      BLACK 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 3   M     RACE      WHITE 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 4   M     RACE      BLACK 10 (25%) 10 (25%)

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: Two analysis variables side side — AVAL (“Value”) PCHG (“Percent Change Baseline”) — canonical “value change” shell. Make variable column band reserved .variable token column. band keys statistic / decimals, bands may carry different (even different-length) stat lists; ragged bands pad NA. Stats rows — column = c(".variable", "<arm>"). variable becomes band arm columns; statistics stack rows cells combined strings. emitted columns named "<variable>..<arm>": Stats columns — column = c(".variable", ".stat"). statistic entry becomes column (landscape shell) arm drops leading row stub. emitted columns named "<variable>..<stat-entry>": reference emitted "<variable>..<arm>" / "<variable>..<stat>" names verbatim manual headers() call draw band spanners — pivot_across() never builds spanners .
code: # AVAL + PCHG by AVISIT x TRTA, built by hand so the example runs without cards. valchg_ard <- tibble::tribble(   ~group1,  ~group1_level, ~group2, ~group2_level, ~variable, ~context,      ~stat_name, ~stat,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "AVAL",    "continuous",  "N",        20,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "AVAL",    "continuous",  "mean",     324,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "AVAL",    "continuous",  "sd",       106,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "AVAL",    "continuous",  "median",   315,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "AVAL",    "continuous",  "N",        20,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "AVAL",    "continuous",  "mean",     318,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "AVAL",    "continuous",  "sd",       98,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "AVAL",    "continuous",  "median",   310,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "PCHG",    "continuous",  "N",        20,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "PCHG",    "continuous",  "mean",     -16,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Drug",        "PCHG",    "continuous",  "sd",       5,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "PCHG",    "continuous",  "N",        20,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "PCHG",    "continuous",  "mean",     -14,   "AVISIT", "DAY 1",       "TRTA",  "Placebo",     "PCHG",    "continuous",  "sd",       5, ) pivot_across(   valchg_ard,   column = c(".variable", "TRTA"),   row_group = "AVISIT",   statistic = list(     AVAL = c(N = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})", Median = "{median}"),     PCHG = c(N = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})")   ),   decimals = list(AVAL = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, median = 1), PCHG = c(mean = 1, sd = 2)) ) #>   AVISIT stat_label     AVAL..Drug AVAL..Placebo   PCHG..Drug PCHG..Placebo #> 1  DAY 1          N             20            20           20            20 #> 2  DAY 1  Mean (SD) 324.0 (106.00) 318.0 (98.00) -16.0 (5.00)  -14.0 (5.00) #> 3  DAY 1     Median          315.0         310.0         <NA>          <NA> pivot_across(   valchg_ard,   column = c(".variable", ".stat"),   row_group = "AVISIT",   statistic = list(     AVAL = c(N = "{N}", Mean = "{mean}", SD = "{sd}"),     PCHG = c(N = "{N}", Mean = "{mean}")   ),   decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2) ) #>   AVISIT    TRTA AVAL..N AVAL..Mean AVAL..SD PCHG..N PCHG..Mean #> 1  DAY 1    Drug      20      324.0   106.00      20      -16.0 #> 2  DAY 1 Placebo      20      318.0    98.00      20      -14.0

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: -arm comparison (difference, hazard ratio, p-value) pivot main ARD’s rows — separate ARD (e.g. cardx). Bind aux =, aligned 1:1 row_group key; entry name becomes column. One aux entry one column; add entries (estimate p-value) several comparison columns. auxiliary ARD must reduce one row per row_group key — many--many alignment aborts rather fabricate rows.
code: resp_main <- tibble::tribble(   ~group1,  ~group1_level, ~group2, ~group2_level, ~variable, ~context,     ~stat_name, ~stat,   "PARAM",  "ORR",         "TRTA",  "Exp",         "AVAL",    "continuous", "mean",     2.2,   "PARAM",  "ORR",         "TRTA",  "Ctl",         "AVAL",    "continuous", "mean",     1.9,   "PARAM",  "DCR",         "TRTA",  "Exp",         "AVAL",    "continuous", "mean",     2.4,   "PARAM",  "DCR",         "TRTA",  "Ctl",         "AVAL",    "continuous", "mean",     2.0, ) diff_ard <- tibble::tribble(   ~group1, ~group1_level, ~variable, ~context,     ~stat_name, ~stat,   "PARAM", "ORR",         "d",       "continuous", "mean",     0.12,   "PARAM", "DCR",         "d",       "continuous", "mean",     0.20, )  pivot_across(   resp_main,   column = "TRTA",   row_group = "PARAM",   statistic = list(continuous = "{mean}"),   aux = list(     "Difference" = list(ard = diff_ard, statistic = "{mean}", decimals = c(mean = 2))   ) ) #>   PARAM variable stat_label Exp Ctl Difference #> 1   ORR     AVAL       AVAL 2.2 1.9       0.12 #> 2   DCR     AVAL       AVAL 2.4 2.0       0.20

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: one thing get right: key statistic ARD’s context. Inspect unique(ard$context) key list match (pass single string / default =). explicitly-supplied statistic matches context, pivot_across() warns rather silently falling back {n}.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: emit() writes file, dispatching extension: returns written path invisibly, emit chainable scripted batch runs. One spec, backend: as_grid(spec) resolves fully-laid-grid without writing file — useful testing programmatic inspection. grid carries resolved pages plus metadata block (pagination counts, resolved column names, effective preset):
code: emit(spec, "table.rtf") # RTF emit(spec, "table.html") # HTML emit(spec, "table.docx") # Word emit(spec, "table.pdf") # PDF (via LaTeX) emit(spec, "table.md") # Markdown data(cdisc_saf_demo, package = "tabular") spec <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "")   )  path <- emit(spec, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")) file.exists(path) #> [1] TRUE grid <- as_grid(spec) length(grid@pages) #> [1] 1 grid@metadata$col_names #> [1] "variable"   "stat_label" "placebo"    "drug_50"    "drug_100"   #> [6] "Total"

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: One spec renders every backend, page-oriented features differ: ¹ HTML/MD one continuous document; browser repeats <thead> print. ² HTML/MD per-page N renders row subgroup banner instead repeating header.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: RTF, HTML, DOCX, Markdown need nothing beyond R package. PDF system dependency — LaTeX engine plus packages backend uses (tabularray, ninecolors, siunitx, tex-gyre, …). Check install: OS-managed TeX Live (RHEL/dnf, Debian/apt): tlmgr locked refuses install (“likely destroy … TeXLive install”). force --ignore-warning. Install user-space TinyTeX control instead: tinytex::install_tinytex(), restart R, tlmgr_install(...). decimal alignment paper backends, metric-compatible fonts matter — check check_fonts(spec).
code: check_latex() # reports what's missing + the exact command tinytex::tlmgr_install(c("tabularray", "ninecolors", "siunitx", "tex-gyre"))

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: relative DOCX path works. emit(spec, "/x.docx") resolves path working directory like every backend (output path absolutised OOXML zip staged). normalizePath() dance needed. PDF build appears hang, LaTeX engine stopping interactive error prompt — fix underlying LaTeX dependency (run check_latex()); render RTF/HTML keep working meantime.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: package ships qualification (inst/qualification/) rebuilds representative CDISC-pilot tables (demographics, populations, AE overview, AE SOC/PT) public PHUSE Test Data Factory ADaM renders every backend, checking three things per cell: emits without error, file structurally valid, independent count computed ADaM appears rendered text backend (cross-backend content parity). four pilot tables across four file backends give 16/16 PASS matrix: ¹ PDF column verified manually local environment LaTeX engine (check_latex()) pdftotext text-parity check. run continuous integration, carries TeX install; CI exercises RTF, HTML, DOCX backends. runnable script (inst/qualification/qualify_tabular_cdisc.R) fetch data qualification README inst/qualification/ folder; direct evidence one tabular spec produces consistent, correct output across backends.

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: Multi-line titles static footnotes arguments tabular():   Table 14-2.01 Demographic Baseline Characteristics ITT Population   Percentages based number ITT subjects per arm. BMI = body mass index. anchored footnote (auto-numbered superscript specific cell header) use footnote() cells_*() location: Excludes one subject withdrawn dosing.
code: tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14-2.01",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "ITT Population"   ),   footnotes = c(     "Percentages are based on the number of ITT subjects per arm.",     "BMI = body mass index."   ) ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal")) base |>   footnote(     "Excludes one subject withdrawn before dosing.",     .at = cells_headers(j = "Total")   )

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: Regulatory TFLs carry header every page (protocol, page X Y, data-cut). preset page chrome, title. pagehead / pagefoot take left / right (center) vectors; tokens {page}, {npages}, {program}, {datetime} resolve render time: running header/footer page chrome — repeats every page paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX) appear single-page HTML preview . Emit paged backend see : Stacking direction. vector stacks outward table: index 1 line nearest table body, later elements move toward page edge. header reads “Analysis Set” top “Protocol” just table, put "Protocol…" first. (Easy invert — check rendered page.)
code: chrome <- base |>   preset(     pagehead = list(       left = c("Analysis Set: Safety", "Protocol: XYZ-123"),       right = c("Data cut: 2026-01-15", "Page {page} of {npages}")     )   ) chrome emit(chrome, "demographics.pdf") # protocol + page x of y on every page

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: style() appends one location + attribute layer. Target region cells_*() constructor; attributes include bold, italic, underline, color, background, borders via brdr(): Body filters live cells_body(): = (row index), j = (column name), = (expression data). border attribute takes brdr() value — width, line style, colour. hairline rule separates pooled Total column per-arm columns: border styles four sides ; border_top / border_bottom / border_left / border_right target one. table-wide rules (toprule, midrule, bottomrule) preset territory — see rules knob preset().
code: base |>   # the column-header band is bold by default, so style it with colour /   # background for a visible change rather than re-applying bold   style(color = "#1F3B5C", background = "#DBE4F0", .at = cells_headers()) |>   style(italic = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |> # italic section rows   style(background = "#F2F2F2", .at = cells_body(j = "Total")) # shade the Total column base |>   style(     border_left = brdr("hairline", "solid", "#ADB5BD"),     .at = cells_body(j = "Total")   )

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: Plain strings render plain text — stray ** never silently bolds surrounding span. Wrap string md() (CommonMark plus ^sup^ / ~sub~) html() (constrained inline tag set) opt , convention gt uses. Every string slot accepts : titles, footnotes, col_spec() labels, style() pretext / posttext:   Table 14-2.01 Demographic Baseline Characteristics ITT Population   BMI = body mass index (kg/m2). Source: ADSL, data cut 15JAN2026. marked string survives c() concatenation, plain marked lines mix freely one titles footnotes vector. Raw HTML inside md() passes tag whitelist html(); tags outside drop wrapper keep text.
code: tabular( cdisc_saf_demo, titles = c( "Table 14-2.01", "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics", md("*ITT Population*") ), footnotes = c( md("BMI = body mass index (kg/m^2^)."), html("Source: ADSL, data cut 15JAN2026.") ) ) |> cols( variable = col_spec( usage = "group", group_display = "header_row", label = "" ), stat_label = col_spec(label = "") ) |> cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal"))

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: preset() carries cosmetic defaults — fonts, rules, padding, na_text, paper/orientation/margins — width_mode decides table fills page: Use set_preset() top study make defaults apply every table without restating . Decimal alignment (col_spec(align = "decimal")) pads numeric cells non-breaking spaces decimal points line . Padding measured built-font metrics (preset(decimal_metrics = "afm"), default), alignment exact Courier exact within one padding space proportional fonts Times New Roman Arial. Markdown output pads character count instead — right geometry text medium.
code: base |>   preset(     font_size = 9,     orientation = "landscape",     paper_size = "letter",     margins = c(1, 0.75, 1, 0.75),     width_mode = "window",     na_text = "-"   )

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: reference safety table: usage = "group" parameter stub, indented statistic rows, decimal-aligned arm columns, population BigN folded header cdisc_saf_n.   Table 14.1.1 Demographic Baseline Characteristics Safety Population   Percentages based number subjects per treatment group.
code:
n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  demo_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Percentages are based on the number of subjects per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   )  demo_tbl emit(demo_tbl, "t_14_1_1.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: flat overview: stat_label plain display column carrying high-level flag rows two-space-indented maximum-severity detail rows (indent baked label strings), category sits one line counts.   Table 14.3.0 Overview Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Safety Population
code:
ae_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.0",     "Overview of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events",     "Safety Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     stat_label = col_spec(label = ""),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   )  ae_tbl emit(ae_tbl, "t_14_3_0.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

what: Adverse events by SOC and preferred term
title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: two-level hierarchy: label holds SOC text SOC rows PT text PT rows, indented indent_level; hidden soc / row_type / n_total / soc_n columns ride along partition sort keys. sort_rows() clusters PTs parent SOC orders levels descending frequency.   Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events System Organ Class Preferred Term Safety Population   table long, real deliverable paginates — keeping SOC preferred terms together. Pagination materialises paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX), add paginate() emit one :
code:
aesocpt_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_aesocpt,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     label = col_spec(       label = "SOC / Preferred Term",       indent = "indent_level"     ),     soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))  aesocpt_tbl aesocpt_tbl |>   paginate(keep_together = "soc", continuation = "(continued)") |>   emit("t_14_3_1.pdf")

what: Best overall response and response rates
title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: efficacy companion: group_label synthesises one bold section band per analysis block (best overall response, ORR, CBR, DCR) via header_row, indented response rows beneath. Denominators come cdisc_eff_n.   Table 14.2.1 Best Overall Response Response Rates Efficacy Evaluable Population
code:
ne <- stats::setNames(cdisc_eff_n$n, cdisc_eff_n$arm_short)  resp_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_eff_resp,   titles = c(     "Table 14.2.1",     "Best Overall Response and Response Rates",     "Efficacy Evaluable Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     group_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Response"),     groupid = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={ne['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={ne['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={ne['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     )   )  resp_tbl emit(resp_tbl, "t_14_2_1.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

what: A figure in the same chrome
title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: “F” TFL. figure() wraps ggplot, recorded base-R plot, zero-argument drawing function, PNG / JPG file submission chrome table — titles, footnotes, per-page header footer — graph ships alongside tables identical page furniture. companion plot vitals table: mean diastolic blood pressure visits, arm, read straight bundled summary.   Figure 7.1 Mean Diastolic Blood Pressure Visit Safety Population   Means taken summary table; error bars omitted clarity. emit() writes figure backend exactly like table; paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX) plot placed body box halign / valign, list plots emits one figure per page.
code: # Mean (SD) rows from the bundled vitals summary, read as numeric means. diabp <- cdisc_saf_vital[   cdisc_saf_vital$paramcd == "DIABP" &     cdisc_saf_vital$stat_label == "Mean (SD)", ] visit_order <- c("Baseline", "Week 8", "Week 16", "End of Treatment") diabp <- diabp[match(visit_order, diabp$visit), ] mean_of <- function(x) as.numeric(sub(" .*", "", x)) arms <- c(placebo = "Placebo", drug_50 = "Drug 50 mg", drug_100 = "Drug 100 mg")  draw_diabp <- function() {   ys <- do.call(cbind, lapply(names(arms), function(a) mean_of(diabp[[a]])))   matplot(     seq_along(visit_order),     ys,     type = "b",     pch = 16,     lty = 1,     col = seq_along(arms),     xaxt = "n",     xlab = "Visit",     ylab = "Mean DIABP (mmHg)"   )   axis(1, at = seq_along(visit_order), labels = visit_order, cex.axis = 0.8)   legend(     "topright",     legend = unname(arms),     pch = 16,     lty = 1,     col = seq_along(arms),     bty = "n"   ) }  vital_fig <- figure(   draw_diabp,   titles = c(     "Figure 7.1",     "Mean Diastolic Blood Pressure by Visit",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Means are taken from the summary table; error bars omitted for clarity." ) vital_fig emit(vital_fig, "g_7_1.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: Data — produce wide frames cards/cardx ARD pivot_across(). Structure Presentation — column, pagination, styling verbs recipe draws . Output & qualification — cross-backend validation recipes mirror.

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: Every column gets role via col_spec(usage = …). Picking right one single important structural decision: Indentation usage role — separate col_spec(indent = …) argument (fixed integer level, column name per-row depth).   Demographics   cols_apply() attaches one shared col_spec arm columns — use instead repeating cols(placebo = …, drug_50 = …) variable number arms. Indent exactly one source. group_display = "header_row" already indents child rows one level, stub column (stat_label) needs indent — section supplies . (explicit indent host overrides auto-indent rather stacking, indent = 1 still yields single level.) care applies labels pivot_across(), come leading indent baked string: keep -trimws() set indent — don’t double .
code: data(cdisc_saf_demo, package = "tabular") arms <- c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal"))

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: (N=…) denominator goes arm’s header label. Build BigN table interpolate glue:   Demographics   Clinical convention: BigN population denominator (ADSL), number rows domain dataset — compute population, summarised data. variable number arms, per-arm label one cols_apply() call instead hand-written line : {.name} token resolves matched column’s name, rest {…} evaluates calling environment, BigN looks :   Demographics
code:
data(cdisc_saf_n, package = "tabular") N <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = ""),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
(N={N['placebo']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
(N={N['drug_50']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
(N={N['drug_100']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
(N={N['Total']})", align = "decimal")   ) arm_cols <- c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(     arm_cols,     col_spec(label = "{.name}
(N={N[.name]})", align = "decimal")   )

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: headers() builds spanning bands groups columns:   Demographics   Widths: "auto" (default) sizes content; pinned value ("1in", 1.0, "20%") wraps within width. Set shared arm width via cols_apply() last — non-default width wins field-merge; later cols() call carrying default width = "auto" otherwise ambiguous.
code: tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "", width = "2.2in")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal", width = "1in")) |>   headers("Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"))

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: Display cells formatted strings — "54 (21.3)" sorts lexically, numerically. idiom: carry one hidden numeric key per sort level, hide col_spec(visible = FALSE), hand keys sort_rows(). descending takes one value per key, mixed-direction sorts single call. bundled AE table ships keys precomputed: soc_n (events parent SOC, constant SOC block) n_total (events row). Sorting , descending, clusters every preferred term SOC orders levels frequency — standard SAP ordering:   AEs SOC PT, descending frequency   soc_n constant within SOC block never smaller PT’s n_total inside , SOC’s summary row sorts top block — separate “parent first” switch needed.
code: data(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, package = "tabular")  tabular(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, titles = "AEs by SOC and PT, descending frequency") |>   cols(     label = col_spec(       label = "SOC / Preferred Term",       indent = "indent_level"     ),     soc = col_spec(       usage = "group",       visible = FALSE,       group_display = "column_repeat"     ),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal")) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: paginate() derives rows-per-page budget preset (paper, font, margins) title/footnote/header line counts — never set rows-per-page hand. keep_together stops page break landing inside section’s run:   AEs SOC PT   preview one continuous table: row pagination, keep_together, continuation marker materialise paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX), HTML. Emit one see page breaks:
code: data(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, package = "tabular") ae_pages <- tabular(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, titles = "AEs by SOC and PT") |>   cols(     label = col_spec(       label = "SOC / Preferred Term",       indent = "indent_level"     ),     soc = col_spec(       usage = "group",       visible = FALSE,       group_display = "column_repeat"     ),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   paginate(     keep_together = "soc",     orphan_floor = 4,     widow_floor = 2,     continuation = "(continued)"   ) ae_pages emit(ae_pages, "ae_soc_pt.pdf") # continuation marker repeats on each continued page

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: columns don’t fit one page, paginate(panels = N) splits non-group columns N chunks repeats every group/id column panel (row labels reappear). Make row label usage = "id" rides every panel:   Demographics (wide split)   Panels paged-backend feature: HTML Markdown table stays one continuous block (preview ), RTF, PDF, DOCX place panel page id / group columns repeated. Emit paged backend see split: Two things know: panels = N splits N equal chunks — explicit split position (“first 5, rest”). Equal split fine page-fit; need specific boundary, known limitation. panels positive integer (default 1 = split). Width-aware automatic splitting planned future feature, current option.
code: wide_split <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics (wide split)") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "") # repeats on every panel   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal")) |>   paginate(panels = 2, continuation = "(continued)") wide_split emit(wide_split, "demographics_wide.pdf") # panel 2 carries the (continued) marker

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: subgroup() partitions table — one page block per value, banner hard page break. partition-constant column can ride banner:   Vital signs sex   different (N=) per arm page (column headers re-resolving per subgroup), pass big_n — small table N per page × arm. bundled dataset carries per-arm-per-page counts, build inline (also shape big_n expects):   Vital signs sex   big_n accepts wide shape (page column + one column per arm) long count()-style table (page, arm, n).
code: data(cdisc_saf_subgroup, package = "tabular") tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital signs by sex") |>   cols(     sex = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     sex_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     visit = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Visit"),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Statistic")   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   subgroup(by = "sex", label = "Sex: {sex} (N = {sex_n})") # page total in banner big_n <- tibble::tribble(   ~sex, ~placebo, ~drug_50, ~drug_100, ~Total,   "F",       53L,      55L,       35L,    143L,   "M",       33L,      41L,       37L,    111L )  tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital signs by sex") |>   cols(     sex_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     visit = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Visit"),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Statistic")   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   subgroup(by = "sex", label = "Sex: {sex}", big_n = big_n) # per-page (N=) per arm

what: Empty tables: no data to report
title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: table whose data zero rows still renders — full page chrome column headers stay intact, empty-data placeholder takes place body. correct output population produced records (cohort subjects, serious-AE table events), rather error blank page. per-table message tabular(empty_text = ...); set house default every table preset(empty_text = ...). message renders single horizontally centred row table body, first data row sit.   Table 14.1.1 Demographic Baseline Characteristics Safety Population   subjects met inclusion criteria cohort. applies subgroup(): zero-N crossing dropped default, subgroup(..., keep_empty = TRUE) keeps renders banner empty-data page — every level shell appears even one data.
code: # Same demographics shell, but the population filter has left no rows. empty_demo <- cdisc_saf_demo[0, ]  tabular(   empty_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "No subjects met the inclusion criteria for this cohort.",   empty_text = "No data available to report" ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"),     drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50 mg", align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100 mg", align = "decimal"),     Total = col_spec(align = "decimal")   )

title: Get started with tabular
text: Start wide frame — bundled demographics summary, one row per statistic, one column per treatment arm: Describe columns. spec prints live HTML table — render emit() produces, shown inline:   Table 14-2.01 Demographic Baseline Characteristics ITT Population   write file, hand spec emit(); backend chosen file extension (explicit format =): whole loop: wide frame → tabular() → cols() → emit() — one spec, backend.
code: data(cdisc_saf_demo, package = "tabular") head(cdisc_saf_demo) #>      variable stat_label     placebo     drug_50    drug_100       Total #> 1 Age (years)          n          86          96          72         254 #> 2 Age (years)  Mean (SD) 75.2 (8.59) 76.0 (8.11) 73.8 (7.94) 75.1 (8.25) #> 3 Age (years)     Median        76.0        78.0        75.5        77.0 #> 4 Age (years)     Q1, Q3  69.2, 81.8  71.0, 82.0  70.5, 79.0  70.0, 81.0 #> 5 Age (years)   Min, Max      52, 89      51, 88      56, 88      51, 89 #> 6  Sex, n (%)          F   53 (61.6)   55 (57.3)   35 (48.6)  143 (56.3) spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14-2.01",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "ITT Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "")   )  spec out <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf") emit(spec, out) # RTF here; swap to .docx / .pdf / .html / .md file.exists(out) #> [1] TRUE

title: Get started with tabular
text: Read left right. summarise upstream — cards/cardx, dplyr, SAS — long ARD, widen display-ready frame pivot_across(), hand tabular. Inside package work happens three phases (Build → Resolve → Emit), resolved spec emits every backend, HTML preview RTF ship can never disagree.  tabular’s pipeline: summarise upstream long ARD, widen pivot_across(), build, resolve, emit one immutable spec every backend.

what: Anatomy of a clinical table page
title: Get started with tabular
text: submission table just grid numbers — page four stacked sections, reviewer expects one place. Every tabular verb maps onto piece picture:  four-section clinical page verb fills section. Header section — running protocol, optional status, page x y, set page chrome preset(pagehead =, pagefoot =). Title lines — table number four centred titles, passed tabular(titles =). Data section — optional subgroup() banner, column-header band built cols() headers(), decimal-aligned data. Footnote lines — static footnotes = plus auto-numbered footnote() markers, program path, name, timestamp. preset() frames four controlling page geometry (paper, orientation, margins, fonts).

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: tabular turns pre-summarised data frame submission-grade clinical table emits natively RTF, PDF, HTML, LaTeX, DOCX — Java, LibreOffice, Word automation. One short pipeline gives decimal alignment via real font metrics, multi-level column headers, predicate-targeted styling, group-aware pagination, built CDISC ADaM workflows FDA / EMA / PMDA submissions. R table package pairs live HTML preview paginated print deliverable: spec eyeball notebook one paginates RTF ship. Scope. tabular renders full set clinical outputs – tables, listings, figures (“T”, “L”, “F” TFL) – RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF, DOCX one verb pipeline. zero-row table renders empty-data placeholder (“data available report”) body, page chrome column headers intact.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub: R dependencies install automatically. five backends differ else need: PDF backend shells . Install tinytex per machine tabular compiles xelatex thereafter: check_latex() reports LaTeX packages present prints exact tlmgr_install() line anything missing; check_fonts(spec) fonts spec asks , per backend. TeX Live managed OS. TeX Live came system package manager (RHEL dnf, Debian/Ubuntu apt), tlmgr usually locked tlmgr_install() fails permissions. Install user-space TinyTeX alongside rather fighting system copy — never reach --ignore-warning force .
code: install.packages("tabular") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("vthanik/tabular") # or remotes::install_github("vthanik/tabular") install.packages("tinytex") tinytex::install_tinytex() # one-time TeX setup tinytex::tlmgr_install(c("tabularray", "ninecolors", "siunitx", "tex-gyre")) tabular::check_latex()   # PDF readiness, with the install remedy

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: pipeline starts pre-summarised wide data frame (one row = one display row — tabular aggregation) chains one verb per concern. Every verb returns updated, immutable tabular_spec; engine resolves render time. tab emits every backend one spec. table tabular’s HTML render — identical spec also produces RTF, paginated PDF, tabularray LaTeX fragment, native OOXML .docx:
code: library(tabular)  # BigN denominators, keyed by arm n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  # columns render in data-frame order, so put them in dose order first; # subset to Age / Sex / Race for a compact display keep <- c("Age (years)", "Sex, n (%)", "Race, n (%)") demo <- cdisc_saf_demo[   cdisc_saf_demo$variable %in% keep,   c("variable", "stat_label", "placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total") ]  tab <- tabular(   demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Percentages are based on the number of subjects per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo (N={n['placebo']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50 (N={n['drug_50']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100 (N={n['drug_100']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total (N={n['Total']})", align = "decimal")   )  # render to any backend by file extension (or format = "...") path <- emit(tab, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")) # submission deliverable

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: Five native backends, one spec. emit() dispatches file extension RTF 1.9.1, PDF (via tinytex), self-contained Bootstrap HTML, tabularray LaTeX, native OOXML DOCX. JVM, Office round-trip. Decimal alignment survives page. Numbers align decimal using backend’s real font metrics, guessed padding — columns stay aligned print, just screen. Submission chrome built . Multi-line titles, eleven footnote lines, page header/footer slots, four-section page layout regulatory reviewers expect. Auto-numbered footnotes. footnote() anchors marker cell, header, title; engine assigns glyph , reading order, deduped id, byte-identical across every backend page. Group-aware pagination. Keep SOC preferred terms one page, repeat titles/headers/footnotes per page, control orphan/widow rows, split wide tables horizontal panels. Display-design. tabular styles renders; never filters, aggregates, weights. Pair cards / gtsummary / dplyr / SAS upstream feed tidy wide frame. QC trail. emit(data_file = ...) writes resolved wide data beside render, CDISC ARS audit manifest documents display.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: tabular renderer pre-summarised clinical tables, statistics engine. Compute summary upstream — cards, gtsummary, dplyr, SAS — hand finished wide frame tabular(). Reach gtsummary rtables want package compute summary; reach tabular render summary already submission-grade output. matrix reflects package’s documented export surface (verified namespaces; via gt means gtsummary renders gt): Two notes marks: Live HTML preview means table renders HTML inline print Quarto / R Markdown chunk RStudio viewer (knit_print method). rtables prints monospace ASCII table default ships knit_print method, — ; can still emit HTML explicit as_html() call. PDF compiled LaTeX, needs TeX installation — see Installation . Every backend pure R.

Tabular's HTML backend emits full <!DOCTYPE html> document <style> block head table inside <body>. inline embedding extract <style> <body> content separately re- wrap htmltools::tagList:   wrapping <div> gets random unique id (multiple tables page CSS-scopable hooks) overflow-x: auto wide tables get horizontal scrollbar instead overflowing container.

title: Convert a tabular_spec to an htmltools tagList — as.tags.tabular_spec
text: Demographics
code: # `as.tags()` converts a spec into an htmltools tagList you can drop into # a custom HTML page, a Shiny UI, or a Quarto / Rmd chunk. `print()` and # `knit_print()` call it under the hood, so you seldom call it directly -- # but it is the seam for composing several tables into one container. s1 <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") s2 <- tabular(cdisc_saf_ae, titles = "AE overall")  # Compose two tables into one parent tagList. Autoprinting `tables` in a # Quarto / Rmd chunk renders both inline (via knit_print); embed it with # htmltools::save_html() or a Shiny renderUI(). tables <- htmltools::tagList(   htmltools::as.tags(s1),   htmltools::as.tags(s2) )  # The common path is autoprinting a spec: the viewer at an interactive # prompt, an inline live table under pkgdown / knitr, and HTML source # under R CMD check. This is the gt / flextable / tinytable convention -- # end on a bare table object and let the registered print method choose, # with no browsable() / if (interactive()) wrapper, so R CMD check never # launches a browser. s1  #tabular-30edc9b813 { font-family: "Courier New", Courier, "Liberation Mono", monospace; what: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Runs full engine pipeline spec returns resolved tabular_grid — intermediate object emit() hands backend. Pure function: files written, global state touched. Use development inspect emit() pass downstream, building custom backend, piping resolved grid non-file consumer (e.g. inline preview chunk Quarto notebook).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code: as_grid(.spec)

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: .spec tabular_spec resolve. <tabular_spec>: required. Built verb chain (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset()).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: tabular_grid S7 object. Two slots: @pages — list one entry per display page. entry named list pagination fields (page_index, panel_index, is_continuation, continuation, show_titles, repeat_headers, show_footnotes_here), row + column slice indices (row_indices, col_indices, col_names), sliced cell text (cells_text — character matrix), sliced inline ASTs (cells_ast — list-matrix inline_ast), sliced style nodes (cells_style — list-matrix style_node), column-label ASTs visible columns (col_labels_ast). @metadata — per-table information backends consume per render: format (resolved backend tag, NA_character_ as_grid() calls), rows_per_page, total_pages, total_panels, nrow_data, ncol_data, col_names, cols (original col_spec() entries keyed column name), headers (flattened header band grid), titles, footnotes, titles_ast, footnotes_ast, col_labels_ast, pagehead_ast / pagefoot_ast (resolved page-band content — NULL active preset declares band, otherwise list(left, center, right) length-N lists inline_ast N = row count index 1 body-edge row).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Engine pipeline order load-bearing. Phases run fixed order; order matters phase reads post- previous-phase state spec: engine_sort() — apply sort spec. engine_headers() — validate header tree flatten band grid. engine_style() — evaluate every style predicate post-sort data grid. predicate may reference column spec@data. engine_format() — apply per-column formats, substitute na_text, parse every cell / title / footnote / label .parse_inline() inline_ast. engine_decimal() — column-wide decimal alignment column flagged col_spec(align = "decimal"). Operates formatted text; output character matrix NBSP padding inserted decimal marks line . engine_paginate() — split pages (vertical row chunks + horizontal panel chunks). plan drives per-page slicing cells / styles / ASTs . grid backend contract. Every backend (backend_md, future backend_html, etc.) consumes tabular_grid — never tabular_spec. New backends need walk grid@pages grid@metadata; engine pipeline fixed dependency never re-implement. /O. as_grid() writes nothing disk touches global state. safe call repeatedly interactive exploration; cost roughly one emit() without backend write step.

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics — inspect the resolved grid ---- # # Resolve the canonical safety-pop demographics pipeline into a # `tabular_grid` and inspect what `emit()` would hand a backend. # The first page's `cells_text` matrix is the decimal-aligned # output as the backend would render it; the metadata carries the # pagination plan + header / title / footnote ASTs. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  demo <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Source: ADSL." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))  demo_grid <- as_grid(demo) demo_grid@metadata$total_pages #> [1] 1 demo_grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text[1:3, c("stat_label", "placebo")] #>      stat_label    placebo       #> [1,] "Age (years)" ""            #> [2,] "  Mean (SD)" "75.2 (8.59)" #> [3,] "  Median"    "76.0       "  # ---- Example 2: AE-by-SOC/PT paginated grid — verify the split ---- # # Same shape as Example 1 plus pagination protecting the SOC # grouping. With a tight font size the grid carries multiple page # entries; concatenating each page's `row_indices` reconstructs # the full data, and every page carries the full header band grid # at `grid@metadata$headers` so backends can re-render the header # on every continuation page. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  ae_spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_aesocpt,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(usage = "group", visible = FALSE,                         group_display = "column_repeat"),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE)) |>   paginate(keep_together = "soc")  ae_grid <- as_grid(ae_spec) length(ae_grid@pages) #> [1] 3  # ---- Example 3: Subgroup partition — one page set per group ---- # # When `subgroup()` is attached, `as_grid()` runs the resolve # pipeline once per group and concatenates the pages. `cdisc_saf_subgroup` # carries `sex` as a natural partition axis; inspect # `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_index` and `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_line_ast` # to confirm each page knows its group identity and banner text. # `sex` auto-hides as the partition `by` column; no explicit # `col_spec(visible = FALSE)` needed. sg_spec <- tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup) |>   cols(     sex_n      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     visit      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Visit"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic")   ) |>   subgroup("sex")  sg_grid <- as_grid(sg_spec) length(sg_grid@pages) #> [1] 2 vapply(   sg_grid@pages,   function(p) if (is.null(p$subgroup_index)) NA_integer_ else p$subgroup_index,   integer(1) ) #> [1] 1 2  # ---- Example 4: Pre-flight inspection before emit() ---- # # Resolve a spec to its grid without writing anywhere. Useful in # tests, for snapshotting cell text under different presets, or # for spec-introspection inside higher-level wrappers that need # to know how many pages a render will produce. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) demog_spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = "Demographics" ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50    = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100   = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total      = col_spec(       label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",       align = "decimal"     )   ) grid <- as_grid(demog_spec) length(grid@pages) #> [1] 1 dim(grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text) #> [1] 16  5

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Build small immutable record describing one border line — width, style, colour. brdr() value stroke hand preset() rules knob (one entry per rule name, e.g. rules = list(midrule = brdr(width = 0.75))) style()'s border arguments (style(border_top = brdr(...), .= cells_table(side = "rows"))). Successive preset() calls layer cleanly, one-override composes onto house-style template without disturbing rules.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
code: brdr(width = "thin", style = "solid", color = "ink")  is_brdr(x)

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: width Stroke width. <numeric(1) | character(1)>: default "thin"*. Either numeric points (>= 0) one four named keywords ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick"`). style Line style. <character(1)>: default "solid"*. One "solid", "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none"`. color Stroke colour. <character(1)>: default "ink"“. "ink" token (resolves `#212529`), hex (`"#RRGGBB"`), CSS colour name, `"currentColor"` inherit surrounding text colour. x R object — tested is_brdr() membership tabular_brdr S3 class.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: tabular_brdr S3 object — length-3 named list suitable preset(rules = list(<rule> = .)) style(border_* = .).

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Surface. single tabular_brdr value length-3 named list class "tabular_brdr": list(style, width, color). shape identical bare triple style()'s per-side scalars accept, resolver R/borders.R can ingest either form transparently. Construct brdr(); test is_brdr(). Width keywords. width accepts either numeric points (typical clinical values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5) one four named keywords: Keywords resolve numeric points immediately; constructed value carries numeric width. Numeric inputs pass unchanged non-negative check. Style enum. style one "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none". "none" explicit clear--rule sentinel: setting rule brdr(style = "none") (bare string "none") preset()(rules = list(...)) suppresses baseline rule backend otherwise draw. Color. Hex ("#212529"), CSS colour name ("black", "slategray"), "ink" token (default; resolves primary rule ink #212529, decoupled surrounding text colour recoloured header keeps neutral rule), "currentColor" (inherit surrounding text colour per backend convention — w:color="auto" DOCX, document text colour RTF, CSS currentColor keyword HTML).

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Table 14.3.1 Overall Summary Adverse Events Safety Population   Subjects counted per category.   Demographics full grid

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — cdisc_eff_estimates
text: Four competing efficacy models treatment-effect point estimate, 95% confidence-interval bounds, nominal p-value. Shaped numeric-cell table (one row per model) rather usual pre-formatted character cells, exercises col_spec(format = ...) + col_spec(na_text = ...) cascade. One row (MMRM) carries NA CI bounds demonstrate na_text.

code: cdisc_eff_estimates

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — cdisc_eff_estimates
text: data frame 4 rows 5 columns: model Model name ("ANCOVA", "MMRM", "Cox PH", "Bootstrap (1000 reps)"). estimate Numeric point estimate. lower_ci, upper_ci Numeric 95% CI bounds. MMRM row NA bounds. p_value Nominal p-value (numeric).

what: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
text: Pre-summarised efficacy table. Per-arm counts best overall response (BOR) per CDISC category, plus derived ORR, CBR, DCR rate rows followed exact (Clopper-Pearson) 95% CI row. Four sections (Best Overall Response, Objective Response Rate, Clinical Benefit Rate, Disease Control Rate) encoded via groupid + group_label pair single usage = "group" / group_display = "header_row" group_label synthesises one bold section band per groupid block; body rows render band, auto-indented one level "header_row" section (stub needs indent — section supplies ).

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
code: cdisc_eff_resp

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
text: data frame 13 rows 7 columns: stat_label Row label ("CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "ORR (CR + PR)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "CBR (CR + PR + SD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "DCR (CR + PR + SD + NON-CR/NON-PD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)"). row_type "category" BOR categorical rows, "derived" ORR / CBR / DCR rate rows, "ci" paired confidence-interval rows. Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). placebo, drug_50, drug_100 Per-arm cell text ("n (pct)" rate rows, "(lower, upper)" CI rows). groupid Integer section id (1 = Best Overall Response, 2 = Objective Response Rate, 3 = Clinical Benefit Rate, 4 = Disease Control Rate). Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE); used section sort / partition key. group_label Character section label, repeating across every row groupid block ("Best Overall Response" x7, "Objective Response Rate" x2, ...). Drives engine's usage = "group" header_row synthesis paired group_display = "header_row".

what: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
text: Pre-summarised AE--SOC/PT table. Interleaved row order: overall "TEAE" row first, per-SOC blocks SOC row followed preferred-term detail rows. Top 10 SOCs top 5 PTs per SOC kept; row_type marks role row indent_level carries canonical depth (0 overall SOC, 1 PT) downstream pipeline drives SOC -> PT indent via col_spec(indent = "indent_level") without reconstructing every script. richer SOC × PT slice exercises paginate() engine's horizontal-panel splitter end--end realistic submission shell.

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
code: cdisc_saf_aesocpt

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
text: data frame 61 rows 10 columns: soc System Organ Class label. Repeats across SOC's PT rows; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE) label carries SOC text SOC rows. label row's display label. Equal soc overall SOC-summary rows; equal preferred-term name PT detail rows. Promoted primary display column — pair indent = "indent_level" drive SOC -> PT indent. row_type One "overall", "soc", "pt". Partition marker; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). indent_level Integer depth (0 overall SOC rows, 1 PT rows). Consumed col_spec(indent = "indent_level") label column; engine auto-hides column resolve time. n_total Integer. row's subject count — overall TEAE count overall row, SOC's count SOC row, PT's count PT row. Inner sort key. soc_n Integer. parent SOC's count, broadcast every row SOC's cluster (SOC row + PT children) descending sort soc_n keeps PTs grouped parent. overall row, equal overall TEAE count. Outer sort key. placebo Placebo arm cell text ("n (pct)"). drug_50, drug_100 Drug arms cell text. Total Pooled-across-arms cell text.

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
text: Derived data-raw/bundle-demo.R pharmaverseadam::adae. Filtered top 10 SOCs total incidence top 5 PTs per SOC. Body rows pre-sorted cards-style two-level rule (arrange(desc(soc_n), soc, desc(n_total))) canonical render order already baked ; render-time sort_rows(= c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE)) reproduces via stable sort.

Total Pooled-across-arms cell text.

title: Cards ARD for demographics (flat ARD companion) — cdisc_saf_demo_ard
text: demographics summary cdisc_saf_demo, long Analysis Results Data (ARD) format produced cards::ard_stack(). One row per (treatment arm, variable, statistic). Shipped teaching dataset shows upstream shape users typically start cards. Convert wide form tabular() accepts via pivot_across() — tabular consume long ARD format, since pre-summarised wide data package boundary.

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: Per-arm subject counts (BigN) safety population, plus Total row. Use table embed BigN inline column headers glue-style {expr} template cols(col_spec(label = ...)); dedicated BigN field col_spec denominator already lives discoverable, joinable form.

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
code: cdisc_saf_n

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: data frame 4 rows 3 columns: arm Raw pharmaverseadam arm label ("Placebo", "Xanomeline Low Dose", "Xanomeline High Dose", "Total"). Matches group1_level _card ARDs (pivot output's column names match setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm) lookup). arm_short Renamed label ("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"). Matches column names cdisc_saf_demo, cdisc_saf_ae, cdisc_saf_aesocpt, cdisc_saf_vital. n Integer subject count.

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: Derived data-raw/bundle-demo.R pharmaverseadam::adsl filtered SAFFL == "Y" three CDISCPILOT01 arms.

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: Two arm-naming columns shipped side side table can serve _card ARDs (raw pharmaverseadam labels group1_level) renamed wide datasets (snake-cased arm column names).

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
code:
# Use cdisc_saf_n$arm_short when joining into the wide datasets # (cdisc_saf_demo, cdisc_saf_ae, cdisc_saf_aesocpt, cdisc_saf_vital). n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}")@label #> [1] "Placebo
N=86"  # Use cdisc_saf_n$arm when joining into pivot_across() output # (column names match the raw pharmaverseadam arm labels). n_arm <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm) col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n_arm['Placebo']}")@label #> [1] "Placebo
N=86"

what: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Pre-summarised vital-signs stats partitioned sex (F / M) across four visits (Baseline, Week 8, Week 16, End Treatment). Two parameters (Systolic BP, Diastolic BP) emit four statistic rows (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). partition-constant sex_n BigN column rides alongside banners can inline denominator via subgroup(label = "Sex: {sex} (N = {sex_n})") without reaching separate lookup.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
code: cdisc_saf_subgroup

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: data frame 64 rows 10 columns: sex Factor (F / M). sex_n Integer BigN — number subjects partition row's sex (partition-constant; rides banner via {sex_n} template tokens). paramcd CDISC parameter code (SYSBP / DIABP). param Decoded parameter name ("Systolic BP (mmHg)", "Diastolic BP (mmHg)"). visit Analysis visit (Baseline, Week 8, Week 16, End Treatment). stat_label Statistic label (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). placebo, drug_50, drug_100, Total Per-arm cell text.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Derived data-raw/bundle-demo.R pharmaverseadam::advs filtered SAFFL == "Y", three CDISCPILOT01 arms, SYSBP / DIABP parameters, four scheduled visits.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex, by Visit — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Designed subgroup() as_grid() examples: partition sex (one page set per sex) nest parameter visit inside page canonical -visit CSR shape, cross sex visit multi-variable partition.

title: Vital-signs summary — cdisc_saf_vital
text: Pre-summarised vital-signs stats. Four parameters (SYSBP, DIABP, PULSE, TEMP) four visits (Baseline, Week 8, Week 16, End Treatment), producing four statistic rows (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). 4 x 4 x 4 grid makes dataset natural fit paginate() examples — 64 rows comfortably exceed single page typical clinical row-per-page settings.
