title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: Build tabular_location value naming one region rendered table; pass result style()'s .argument. constructor targets one surface (body, headers, footnotes, ...); optional / j / / level / labels filters narrow target within surface.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
code: cells_body(i = NULL, j = NULL, where = NULL)  cells_headers(level = NULL, labels = NULL, j = NULL)  cells_group_headers(j = NULL, where = NULL)  cells_title()  cells_subgroup_labels()  cells_footnotes()  cells_pagehead(slot = NULL)  cells_pagefoot(slot = NULL)  cells_table(side = NULL, i = NULL, j = NULL)  is_tabular_location(x)

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: Row index filter. <integer | logical | character | NULL>. Integer = 1-based row numbers; logical = length-nrow mask (broadcasts scalar TRUE/FALSE); character = matches visible row labels. NULL (default) = filter (every row). j Column index filter. <integer | character | NULL>. Integer = 1-based column positions; character = matches column names spec@data. NULL (default) = every column. Predicate. unquoted expression evaluating length-nrow logical vector run data grid. Captured rlang quosure (pvalue < 0.05 works without needing wrap vars() similar). Mutually exclusive . level Header-band depth (cells_headers). <integer(1) | NULL>. 1 = topmost spanner band; increasing integers walk toward leaves. -1 = leaf band (per-column labels built col_spec@label). NULL (default) = every band every depth. labels Header-band labels (cells_headers). <character | NULL>. Targets header_node(s) whose @label matches, depth. Mutually exclusive level. slot Band slot (cells_pagehead / cells_pagefoot). <character(1) | NULL>. One "left", "center", "right", NULL every slot. side Table edge / separator (cells_table). <character(1) | NULL>. One "outer" (four outer edges), "outer_top", "outer_bottom", "outer_left", "outer_right", "rows" (horizontal separator body rows), "cols" (vertical separator body columns), NULL whole-body (cells_body()). x R object — tested is_tabular_location() membership tabular_location S3 class.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: tabular_location S3 list slots surface, , j, , labels, level, slot, side (unused slots NULL). Pass style()'s .argument.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: One surface per location. tabular_location always names exactly one : body, headers, group_headers, title, subgroup_labels, footnotes, pagehead, pagefoot, table. Cross-surface styling layers via multiple chained style() calls (one per location). Index vocabulary. supported, (rows) j (columns) arguments accept integer, logical, character vectors — matching convention established flextable (bold(ft, , j)) tinytable (style_tt(, j)). Character vectors match data frame's column names (j) row labels (); integers 1-based positions; logicals broadcast nrow / ncol. Predicate vocabulary. cells_body(= pvalue < 0.05) canonical data-driven filter — captured rlang quosure evaluated engine time post-sort grid. Mutually exclusive (target either index predicate, ). cells_headers cells_column_spanners. verb builds multi-level header tree named headers(). location follows vocabulary: one word ("headers") covers entire column-header section — inner spanner bands leaf band per-column labels. Pass level labels narrow.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
code: # Whole body cells (the default for style()) cells_body() #> <tabular_location: body()>  # Row index 1:3, column "Total" cells_body(i = 1:3, j = "Total") #> <tabular_location: body(i=1,2,3, j=Total)>  # Data-driven subset cells_body(where = stat_label == "Mean (SD)") #> <tabular_location: body(where=stat_label == "Mean (SD)")>  # Topmost spanner band only cells_headers(level = 1) #> <tabular_location: headers(level=1)>  # Leaf band (per-column labels) cells_headers(level = -1) #> <tabular_location: headers(level=-1)>  # A specific spanner by label cells_headers(labels = "Treatment Group") #> <tabular_location: headers(labels=c('Treatment Group'))>  # Section-header rows for col_spec(group_display = "header_row") cells_group_headers() #> <tabular_location: group_headers()>  # Title / footnotes blocks cells_title() #> <tabular_location: title()> cells_footnotes() #> <tabular_location: footnotes()>  # Page-header / page-footer slots cells_pagehead(slot = "left") #> <tabular_location: pagehead(slot='left')> cells_pagefoot(slot = "right") #> <tabular_location: pagefoot(slot='right')>  # Outer table frame cells_table(side = "outer") #> <tabular_location: table(side='outer')>  # Horizontal rules between body rows cells_table(side = "rows") #> <tabular_location: table(side='rows')>

what: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: Walks resolved font fallback chain backend reports entries local machine can find. Useful answering "preview seeing fonts downstream reviewer see?".

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
code: check_fonts(.spec)

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: .spec tabular_spec preset_spec. <tabular_spec | preset_spec>: required. spec whose effective preset determines font chain walk.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: Invisibly returns resolved per-backend chains named list character vectors. Side effect: prints cli tree showing availability marker every entry.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: diagnostic change emit() writes file. Tabular's backends emit font names (CSS strings, LaTeX \setmainfont commands, RTF font-table entries); consuming application (browser, LaTeX engine, Word, Adobe Reader) opening machine resolves names installed fonts. check_fonts() purely informational — tells entries cross-platform fallback chain can see machine, can predict drift. Status markers: v — font installed machine (via systemfonts). o — font CSS / LaTeX generic; always resolvable consuming application. x — font installed machine; consuming app different machine may may . Every backend name-references fonts; consuming application (browser, LaTeX engine, Word, Adobe Reader) resolves names installed fonts. x therefore means reader needs face installed render match exactly. default chains lead ubiquitous Office cores (Courier New / Arial / Times New Roman), v default common case Windows macOS. Requires systemfonts package (Suggests); call install.packages("systemfonts") first installed.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
code: # ---- Example 1: Inspect default font resolution ---- # # Build a spec with the default font_family ("mono") and ask # which entries in the cross-platform chain are findable # locally. Useful before sharing a render with downstream # reviewers who may be on a different OS. spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = "Demographics" ) if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = mono`  #> backend: html #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono #> o monospace (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono #> x TeX Gyre Cursor (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Mono (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono  # ---- Example 2: Diagnose a Courier New request ---- # # A request for "Courier New" (a specific named font) renders # on macOS / Windows but may fall back to a serif on Linux. # `check_fonts()` flags this so the user knows to switch to # the "mono" generic for portable output. spec_mono <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = "Mono request" ) |>   preset(font_family = "Courier New") if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec_mono) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = Courier New`  #> backend: html #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono #> o monospace (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono #> x TeX Gyre Cursor (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Mono (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Courier New (not on this machine) #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Mono  # ---- Example 3: Explicit cross-platform stack ---- # # A length>1 input is treated as an explicit fallback chain and # emitted verbatim — no alias lookup, no fabrication. Use this # when the first choice is a sponsor / brand face that needs an # honest fallback for reviewers who don't have it installed. spec_brand <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo) |>   preset(font_family = c("Inter", "Liberation Sans", "Arial", "sans")) if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec_brand) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = Inter          , Liberation Sans, Arial          , and sans           `  #> backend: html #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> o sans (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> o sans (generic, always available) #> backend: rtf #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> o sans (generic, always available)  # ---- Example 4: Compare serif vs sans fallback chains ---- # # Side-by-side check of the two generic families. Useful when # deciding the house-style default: the serif chain leads with # Liberation Serif (Linux-server-first); the sans chain leads # with Liberation Sans. Both close with the backend's native # fallback layer (CSS generic on HTML, Latin Modern on LaTeX). if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   tabular(cdisc_saf_demo) |>     preset(font_family = "serif") |>     check_fonts()    tabular(cdisc_saf_demo) |>     preset(font_family = "sans") |>     check_fonts() } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = serif`  #> backend: html #> x Times New Roman (not on this machine) #> x Times (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Serif #> o serif (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Times New Roman (not on this machine) #> x Times (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Serif #> x TeX Gyre Termes (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Roman (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Times New Roman (not on this machine) #> x Times (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Serif #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = sans`  #> backend: html #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> x Helvetica (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans #> o sans-serif (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> x Helvetica (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans #> x TeX Gyre Heros (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Sans (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Arial (not on this machine) #> x Helvetica (not on this machine) #> v Liberation Sans

what: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
text: Reports, every TeX package LaTeX / PDF backend can emit, whether present local TeX tree, prints exact tinytex::tlmgr_install() call installs missing. Run emit(spec, ".pdf") fresh machine turn cryptic mid-compile File 'tabularray.sty' found -front, actionable checklist.

title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
code: check_latex(quiet = FALSE)

title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
text: quiet Suppress printed cli report. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE, runs checks returns result invisibly without printing. Use scripts branch return value.

title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
text: Invisibly returns data frame one row per required package columns package (<character>) installed (<logical>, NA undeterminable). Side effect: prints cli report per-package status marker , anything missing, exact tlmgr_install() remedy.

title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
text: required set superset every \\usepackage{} / \\UseTblrLibrary{} directive backend emits, across conditional branches (running headers / footers pull fancyhdr + lastpage; xelatex pulls fontspec; pdflatex pulls classic font bundles). check informational, install anything. OS-managed TeX Live gotcha. Linux distributions ship TeX Live system package manager (RHEL / Fedora via dnf, Debian / Ubuntu via apt), tlmgr locked user installs tlmgr_install() fail. fix install user-space TinyTeX tinytex::install_tinytex() let tree packages. Never force locked tlmgr --ignore-warning: leaves system tree half-written. Slow / stuck install (often Windows). default CTAN repository mirror.ctan.org redirects random mirror every call, slow stale one makes tinytex::tlmgr_install() appear hang. Pin concrete mirror tinytex::tlmgr_repo()("auto") (follows redirect single time remembers result), retry install. Status markers: v — package installed local TeX tree. x — package missing; tlmgr_install() line bottom report installs every missing package . ? — availability determined (tinytex, tlmgr reachable); treated missing remediation. Requires tinytex package (Suggests); call install.packages("tinytex") first installed.

title: Check LaTeX-package availability for PDF output — check_latex
code: # ---- Example 1: Audit the PDF toolchain before emitting ---- # # Run check_latex() on a fresh machine to confirm every LaTeX # package the PDF backend needs is present. The call prints a # status line per package and, if any are missing, the exact # tinytex::tlmgr_install() command to fix them in one shot. It is # guarded on tinytex so it is a no-op where TeX is unavailable. if (requireNamespace("tinytex", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_latex() } #>  #> ── LaTeX packages for PDF output  #> x tabularray #> x ninecolors #> x xcolor #> x graphics #> x siunitx #> x geometry #> x hyperref #> x iftex #> v base #> x fancyhdr #> x lastpage #> x fontspec #> x tex-gyre #> x psnfss #> ! Missing 13 LaTeX packages: "tabularray", "ninecolors", "xcolor", "graphics", "siunitx", "geometry", "hyperref", "iftex", "fancyhdr", "lastpage", "fontspec", "tex-gyre", and "psnfss". #> Install with `tinytex::tlmgr_install(c('tabularray', 'ninecolors', #> 'xcolor', 'graphics', 'siunitx', 'geometry', 'hyperref', 'iftex', #> 'fancyhdr', 'lastpage', 'fontspec', 'tex-gyre', 'psnfss'))`. #> If the install stalls (commonly on Windows, where the default CTAN #> mirror redirects on every call), pin a concrete mirror once with #> `tinytex::tlmgr_repo("auto")` then retry. #> On an OS-managed TeX Live (RHEL/dnf, Debian/apt) tlmgr is locked: #> install a user-space TinyTeX with `tinytex::install_tinytex()` #> instead. Never force a locked tlmgr with `--ignore-warning`.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: Build single column's display attributes — usage, label, format, visibility, width, alignment, NA text. result feeds cols(), stamps input column name onto spec named- argument position attaches parent tabular_spec.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
code: col_spec(   usage = NULL,   label = NA_character_,   format = NULL,   visible = NA,   width = "auto",   group_display = NA,   group_skip = NA,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   na_text = NA_character_,   indent = NA )

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: usage Engine role. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One : "display" (default cols()) — pass-. "group" — row-label repeat-suppression continuation-page repeat keys. Use variable, soc, stat_label. (Cosmetic indent depth separate indent argument, usage role.) "id" — row-identifier column. Renders like "display" (one value per row, never collapses) joins stub: repeats every horizontal panel (paginate(panels = N)) shows left continuous backend (HTML / Markdown) collapses panels one table. PROC REPORT ID role, orthogonal grouping. Use per-row statistic label ("n", "Mean", "SD") must stay legible every panel wide demographics efficacy table. NULL / NA — unset sentinel; resolves "display" render. NA mergeable, explicit "display" later cols() call can override prior "group" / "id".       label Display label column header. <character(1)>: default NA_character_. Embed \n multi-line headers (arm name row 1, BigN denominator row 2 clinical convention). NA_character_ means use input column name verbatim. Restriction: Empty string whitespace-labels accepted , unlike headers() band labels strict. Supports glue-style {expr} interpolation: braces evaluated R code calling environment build time, BigN value folds inline, label = "Placebo (N={n['placebo']})". Double brace ({{ }}) literal one. md() / html() label passed without interpolation. Per-column token. {.name} (alias {.col}) inside {expr} deferred resolved matched column's name spec stamped cols() / cols_apply(), one spec can carry variable-N arm header. See cols_apply() loop-free idiom.   format Post-cell formatter. <character(1) | function | NULL>: default NULL. sprintf template applied per cell, unary function(x) -> character length, NULL backend default. Restriction: Character templates probed sprintf(format, 0) construction; malformed templates fail fast. Tip: Use function non-sprintf formatting (locale- aware numbers, thousand separators, conditional symbols).   visible Whether column renders. <logical(1)>: default NA. FALSE hides column output keeps spec@data sort_rows() style() predicates can still reference . NA (default) merge "unset" sentinel — resolves visible render , crucially, mergeable: later cols() call visible = TRUE can re-show column earlier call hid. Interaction: Hidden columns standard pattern sort-key helpers (row_type, n_total) numeric counts behind formatted-text percentage cells. Auto-hide. depth column named character indent every column named subgroup(= ...) referenced via {col} placeholder subgroup banner template flipped visible = FALSE automatically engine time — restating redundant. Break-group column. hidden usage = "group" column emits header rows -column text; contributes group_skip transitions, group_display ignored hidden. canonical "spacer" drops blank line wherever marker value changes (e.g. continuous stats vs. categorical groups inside one characteristic): col_spec(usage = "group", group_skip = TRUE, visible = FALSE). width Column width — auto-sized, pinned, proportional. <character(1) | numeric(1)>: default "auto". "auto" (default) — engine measures widest cell (header + body) using bundled Adobe AFM Core 13 glyph metrics distributes available content width. header sized widest word, multi-word header (e.g. "n, median") wraps spaces; non-breaking space ( ) keeps run whole. body sized widest line never wraps, numeric values stay intact. Pin numeric width wrap body . <number> — pinned inches. Backends wrap content inside pinned width (tabularray Q[wd=...], HTML style="width:...", RTF / DOCX twips conversion). "2.5in" / "60mm" / "4cm" / "30pt" / "5pc" — pinned dimension explicit TeX unit. behaviour bare numeric. "30%" — proportional width, percent available content width. Resolved engine time printable area. Tip: Mix freely. Pinned percent widths take priority; "auto" columns distribute whatever space remains. pinned widths together exceed available content width, engine warns leaves "auto" columns natural fit (layout may overflow). Restriction: Must positive. Percent values must fall [0, 100]. Font-relative units (em, ex, rem) rejected (font-size context parse time). Cross-format semantics (gt convention). width value user's source--truth. HTML emits verbatim <col style="width:..."> (CSS accepts every unit: %, , px, pt, cm, mm). Paper backends (LaTeX / RTF / PDF / DOCX) convert native unit via AFM / distribute-widths pipeline. HTML unconditionally responsive: width = "auto" (default), browser auto-sizes column cells wrap viewport narrows. Note: NA NULL rejected. pre-v0.1.0 tabular NA deferred backend auto-fit; path inconsistent across backends replaced "auto" default, produces identical widths across RTF / LaTeX / HTML. Merge sentinel. field-merge across repeated cols() / cols_apply() calls, "auto" treated default: later call carrying width = "auto" leaves previously pinned width intact, explicit non-"auto" width overrides. group_display usage = "group" values render body. <character(1)>: default NA. Active usage = "group" — setting non-group column ignored warns. NA (default) merge "unset" sentinel resolves "header_row" render; explicit value mergeable, later cols() call can reset back "header_row". "header_row" (default) — unique value emits section header row block data rows, body rows beneath automatically indented one level. section header sits flush left depth 0; child rows render one indent level . section already supplies indent, stub column needs indent — adding indent = 1 overrides (stack) auto-indent, leaving single level. source column hidden visible body. Matches canonical submission shape used clinical TFL house templates (Disposition, Demographics, Statistical Report sections). group exactly one member collapses single flush-left row: group value becomes row's label, data cells kept, separate header indent emitted (redundant two-line "header plus lone child" gone). standard occurrence-table shape, single binary flag (DEATH, TEAE) sits flush among headed multi-level breakdowns. collapsed row still receives cells_group_headers() styling (preset explicit), group identity lost. empty NA group value never emits header ; members render flush left keeping labels. (Collapse applies single header_row band; nested two-header_row layout keeps header per value.) "column" — column stays visible; repeated values suppressed (first row value shows label). PROC REPORT's default grouping variables. "column_repeat" — column stays visible; every row repeats value (suppression). shape R's print.data.frame produces. Composition multiple group columns. one usage = "group" column declared, FIRST one encountered cols() order outer group; subsequent group columns nest inside . column's group_display choice independent — common clinical pattern outer variable "header_row" plus inner stat_label "column" (visible row labels section header).   group_skip Insert blank row consecutive groups. <logical(1)>: default NA. Active usage = "group" — setting non-group column ignored warns. Three values: TRUE — engine injects one blank row immediately value transition column (PROC REPORT's BREAK var / SKIP semantics, lifted per-column control). Never trails final group. FALSE — never insert blank row column. NA (default) — follow group_display: TRUE group_display = "header_row", FALSE "column" "column_repeat". Picks canonical shape without extra knob set. Interaction: two columns effective group_skip = TRUE value transitions coincide row, engine emits ONE blank row boundary, one per column. Transition row indices unioned across contributing group columns.   align Horizontal alignment within column. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One : "left" — character columns; row labels. "center" — column-header band; rarely data cells. "right" — numeric content without decimals. "decimal" — numeric mixed-format cells aligned decimal mark. Use "5 (3.2%)" next "54 (32.1%)". NULL (default) — falls preset(alignment = list(body_halign = ...)) baked default "left". Tip: "decimal" pads numerics non-breaking spaces decimal mark falls single column-wide anchor. Pad counts follow active preset's decimal_metrics knob (see preset()): default "afm" measures real glyph widths anchor holds proportional fonts well monospace. Default behaviour. align unset (NULL / NA), every column emits body left-aligned header centred, regardless column's R data type. tabular's canonical input pre-summarised wide data frames numeric content already formatted character strings (e.g. "52 (60.5)"), .numeric()-based auto-detection mis-classify columns text align left — opposite intent. Use explicit align = "decimal" NBSP-padded numeric columns (centred header padded centroid) align = "right" plain right-aligned numeric columns. default cascade body → preset(alignment = list( body_halign = ...)) → CSS text-align: left; header → preset(alignment = list(header_halign = ...)) → CSS text-align: center. valign Vertical alignment within cell. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One "top", "middle", "bottom". NULL falls preset(alignment = list(body_valign = ...)) (baked default "top"). Per-cell overrides via style(valign = ...) still win column setting. Tip: Set "middle" row-label column banded- row table label stays centred multi-line stat-block adjacent cell. na_text Text substituted NA cells. <character(1) | NA>: default NA. Substituted format step, format need anticipate NA. NA (default) inherits preset's table-wide na_text; string overrides column, including "" force blank cells even preset uses non-empty token. Tip: Use sentinel ("-", "NR", ".") blank cells ambiguous, e.g. "applicable" "reported" render blank. indent Cosmetic indent depth column. <numeric(1) | character(1) | NA>: default NA. Two modes type: non-negative whole number — every body row column indented many levels (level preset@indent_size space-widths). indent = 1 common "nudge stub one level" case; indent = 0 real value flattens children "header_row" section. column name (character) — per-row depth: engine reads spec@data[[indent]], coerces row non-negative integer, prefixes row's text + AST strrep(" ", preset@indent_size * depth). referenced depth column auto-hidden — need set visible = FALSE . NA (default) means indent. Backends native padding-left (HTML / LaTeX / RTF / DOCX / PDF) emit depth cell padding wrapped continuation lines align indented baseline; Markdown carries literal space-prefix. Synthesised group-header rows never indented — parent depth 0. Interaction: explicit indent group_display = "header_row" host suppresses section's automatic one-level child indent (take control depth) — stub section needs indent , adding indent = 1 yields single, double, indent. Per-row SOC / PT pattern (bundled cdisc_saf_aesocpt ships canonical depth column, upstream construction needed):   Depth-column values c(0L, 1L, 2L, …) produce 0, 1, 2, … levels. Negative values clamp 0 (warn); fractional numerics floor (warn); NA → 0 (silent). Works flat listings — character indent require usage = "group" columns.
code: # Two row-label columns and four arm columns. cols(   variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),   stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group"),   placebo    = col_spec(),   drug_50    = col_spec() ) # Section-band table: the `group_label` column drives section # headers; `stat_label` body rows auto-indent under each header # without an explicit depth column. cols(   group_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),   stat_label  = col_spec(label = "Response"),   placebo     = col_spec(align = "decimal") ) # End-to-end ARD → wide → tabular pipeline. The cards ARD # `cdisc_saf_demo_ard` is the long upstream input; `pivot_across()` # widens to one column per arm and stamps an internal marker # so [`sort_rows()`] can reject sort keys on those arm columns. # `cols()` then attaches per-column display rules. wide <- pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous  = c(N = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})"),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) ) tabular(wide, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable                 = col_spec(       usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"     ),     stat_label               = col_spec(       usage = "group", label = "Statistic"     ),     Placebo                  = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     `Xanomeline High Dose`   = col_spec(       label = "High Dose", align = "decimal"     ),     `Xanomeline Low Dose`    = col_spec(       label = "Low Dose", align = "decimal"     ),     Total                    = col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) # Two-line header with arm name and BigN from cdisc_saf_n. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) col_spec(   label = "Placebo\nN={n['placebo']}",   align = "decimal" ) # sprintf template vs. function form. col_spec(format = "%.1f") col_spec(format = function(x) formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 1, big.mark = ",")) # Demographics layout: variable as section header, stat_label # as visible suppressed column. cols(   variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),   stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "column"),   placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal") ) # Default: header_row mode auto-injects blanks between sections. col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row")  # Override: keep the column visible (suppressed-value mode) but # still insert blank-row separators between value changes. col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "column", group_skip = TRUE)  # Override: section headers without the blank-row separator # (denser layout, used when vertical space is tight). col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row", group_skip = FALSE)  # Break-only "spacer": pairs with visible = FALSE to drop a blank # line wherever a hidden marker changes, without rendering the # column or any header row. group_display is ignored when hidden. col_spec(usage = "group", group_skip = TRUE, visible = FALSE) cols(   label    = col_spec(label = "Category", indent = "indent_level"),   soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),   row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE) )

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: col_spec S7 object. Pass cols() keyed input column name; constructor stamp name.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: Constructor-. col_spec() know input column belongs cols() stamps name. Build reusable specs ordinary R objects (e.g. arm_col <- col_spec(align = "decimal")) apply multiple inputs without restating name. Merge semantics across repeated cols() calls. cols() called twice column, engine merges field--field: field set non-default value new spec overrides; field left "unset" sentinel (NA / NULL / "auto") leaves existing value intact. every mergeable field genuine unset sentinel, later call can also restore default — e.g. visible = TRUE re-shows column earlier call hid. Build column's spec stages without re-stating earlier attributes. Validation timing. Argument shapes validated eagerly — malformed sprintf template probed construction (sprintf(format, 0)) fails fast write time, render time.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: Table 14.1.1 Demographics Baseline Characteristics Safety Population   Percentages based N per treatment group.   Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events SOC Preferred Term Safety Population     Treatment-effect estimates model     Vital Signs Baseline End Treatment     Table 14.1.1 Demographics Safety Population

# ---- Example 1: Demographics with every col_spec field exercised ---- # # Demographics table where every `col_spec` field is in play: # the row-label columns are pinned to a fixed width and aligned # left, the four arm columns embed BigN inline in the header, # decimal-align numeric content, and render `NA` cells as "-". n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(       usage = "group", label = "Parameter",       width = 2.0,     align = "left"     ),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic", align = "left"),     placebo  = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal", na_text = "-"     ),     drug_50  = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal", na_text = "-"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal", na_text = "-"     ),     Total    = col_spec(       label = "Total

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: Add col_spec() entries tabular_spec. named argument one column: name input column .spec@data value col_spec carrying column's display attributes (usage, label, format, alignment, width, visibility, NA text). Columns mentioned get default col_spec() (usage = display) engine-validate time.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
code: cols(.spec, ..., .default = NULL)

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: .spec tabular_spec extend. <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short user-supplied name (e.g. s, sp) ... spec slot. Pipe input (tabular(...) |> cols(...)) works normal way — spec supplied positionally. ... Named col_spec objects, one per column. name input column name .spec@data. Names must match existing column — pre-compute derived columns upstream dplyr::mutate() (equivalent) tabular(). Restriction: Names must unique within single cols() call (duplicates warn; "last value wins"). Tip: override attribute already declared, use second cols() call downstream let merge rule apply. .default Fallback col_spec unmentioned columns. <col_spec | NULL>: default NULL. col_spec, field-merged onto every data column named ... already carry spec earlier cols() call. NULL (default) leaves unmentioned columns engine-time default. Use set one alignment / format across variable number arm columns single call. Interaction: Explicit ... specs always win — .default fills gaps. column carried prior cols() call treated already specified left untouched.
code: # Decimal-align every arm column without listing each by name. tabular(cdisc_saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     .default   = col_spec(align = "decimal")   )

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining headers(), sort_rows(), style().

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: Sparse declaration. Declare columns whose attributes differ default — typical pipeline uses one cols() call one entry per non-default column. Layout order follows data frame, cols(). left--right column order rendered table column order .spec@data; order named ... arguments irrelevant (lookup keyed name). move column, reorder data frame upstream — column derived df$new <- ... appended last render last unless reorder. Within-call duplicates warn. duplicate name inside one cols() call warns "last value wins". intentionally override attribute, use second cols() call downstream let merge rule apply.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: cols() called column, engine merges new col_spec existing one field-- field. field set non-default value new spec overrides; field left "unset" sentinel leaves existing value intact. Every mergeable field genuine unset sentinel, later call can also restore default (e.g. visible = TRUE re-shows hidden column, group_display = "header_row" resets prior "column"). lets build column's spec stages — declare label--alignment block front, add width know fits, attach sort key, without re-stating earlier attributes. Essential generating specs programmatically (looping arms, layering house-style helper). Unset sentinels — field left value override existing field (every value, including default like visible = TRUE, overrides):
code: # Three-stage build: label/usage first, alignment second, width # third. Each stage leaves earlier fields intact. tabular(cdisc_saf_demo) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter")) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(align = "left")) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(width = 2.0)) # Result: variable has usage="group", label="Parameter", #         align="left", width=2.0 — all four fields set.

what: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
title: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
text: Field-merge single col_spec() onto every column matched name predicate. vectorized companion cols() common case variable number treatment-arm columns share display rule (decimal alignment, numeric format), avoid .call() / !!! splicing one named argument per arm.

title: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
code: cols_apply(.spec, .cols, .col_spec)

title: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
text: .spec tabular_spec extend. <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed partial matching bind user name another slot. .cols Columns match. <character | function>: required. Either character vector input column names .spec@data, predicate function(names) -> logical evaluated names(.spec@data) (one logical per column, length). Restriction: Named columns must exist .spec@data. predicate must return logical vector length names(.spec@data). Tip: tidyselect helpers ship; pass base vector (grep("^ARM", names(df), value = TRUE)) predicate (\(nm) startsWith(nm, "ARM")). .col_spec spec field-merge onto every match. <col_spec>: required. Built col_spec().

title: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining headers(), sort_rows(), style().

title: Apply one column spec to many columns — cols_apply
text: Field-merge, replace. cols_apply() reuses field--field merge repeated cols() calls: non-default field .col_spec overrides; default-valued field leaves prior attribute matched column intact. Set shared rule across arms first, refine individual arm later cols() call (reverse). Per-column label token. label references {.name} (alias {.col}) inside {expr} resolved per matched column, .name .col bound column's name. makes variable-N arm header single declarative call instead hand-written loop. rest {expr} evaluates calling environment, per-arm BigN looked named vector works directly:   token plain-string feature; label wrapped md() / html() parsed eagerly interpolate. failing token expression aborts naming offending column. width merge. width's default sentinel merge "auto": later cols() / cols_apply() call carrying default width = "auto" leaves previously pinned width intact (explicit non-"auto" width overrides). Apply shared width last broadcast across arms.
code:
n <- c(placebo = 86, drug_50 = 84, drug_100 = 84) cols_apply(   spec, c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100"),   col_spec(label = "{.name}
(N={n[.name]})", align = "decimal") ) # placebo  -> "placebo
(N=86)" ; drug_50 -> "drug_50
(N=84)" ; ...

what: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: Resolve spec engine pipeline, dispatch backend registered chosen format, (optionally) write QC data file CDISC ARS audit manifest alongside rendered artefact. emit() package's terminal verb — returns file invisibly call can sit bottom pipe without losing path.

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
code: emit(   .spec,   file,   format = NULL,   data_file = NULL,   manifest = FALSE,   create_dir = FALSE )

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: .spec tabular_spec render. <tabular_spec>: required. full verb chain (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset()) feeds emit()'s first argument pipe. file Destination path rendered artefact. <character(1)>: required. Extension drives backend (see dispatch table Details section). parent directory must already exist; emit() auto-create directories. Tip: Use tempfile(fileext = ".md") inside vignettes examples example runs R CMD check without polluting package directory. format Explicit backend override. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. set, wins file extension. Useful writing .txt files contain RTF, round-trip testing, user custom backend registered non-standard name. data_file QC artefact writer. <character(1) | function(file) -> character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. set, writes resolved wide data frame alongside render. character path writes directly; lambda receives render path returns data file path (typical sponsor-flexible naming). Restriction: Returned-path extension must .csv, .tsv / .txt, .rds. Tip: data frame lambda governs pre-backend — CSV emitted regardless whether file RTF, PDF, DOCX.   manifest Emit CDISC ARS audit manifest sidecar. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE writes <file>.audit.yml verbatim CDISC ARS LDM v1.0 Output keys; see manifest = TRUE invariant Details section file contains determinism contract satisfies. create_dir Create destination directory missing. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE, parent directory file (missing ancestors) created recursively rendering, instead aborting. default FALSE keeps safe behaviour erroring missing parent.
code: # Three canonical sponsor patterns for the lambda. data_file = \(f) paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f), "_qc.csv") data_file = \(f) file.path(   "validation",   paste0("val_", basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f)), ".csv") ) data_file = \(f) file.path(   "rd",   paste0("rd_", basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f)), ".rds") )

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: file path, invisibly. Use chaining emit() downstream consumer needs resolved path (e.g. printing link Quarto chunk, copying sidecar manifest archive, attaching render submission folder builder).

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: Validation /O. Every argument validated backend resolved engine runs. unsupported extension, malformed data_file path, missing backend raises tabular_error_input without writing file. spec resolves cleanly whose backend errors mid-write may leave partial file behind; failure mode touches disk. Backend dispatch. effective backend resolved file extension via table ; format argument always wins supplied. backend lives R/backend_<fmt>.R file self-registers package load time. Unknown extensions, missing extensions, formats registered backend raise tabular_error_input. error message lists currently registered formats failure actionable. data_file sponsor-neutral. Pass explicit path ("/qc.csv") fixed location, lambda (function(file) -> path) sponsor-flexible naming. lambda receives resolved render path can derive QC file (suffix, sibling folder, separate sponsor-styled name). Recognised extensions returned path .csv, .tsv (alias: .txt), .rds; anything else raises tabular_error_input. written data frame post- sort_rows() / post-engine_decimal() wide grid — exactly cell text backend wrote. manifest = TRUE writes sidecar. audit manifest written <file>.audit.yml next render (e.g. .md -> .audit.yml). Keys CDISC ARS LDM v1.0 Output verbatim: id, name, programmingCode (best-effort git + R + platform timestamp), fileSpecifications (sha256 every emitted artefact including data_file), displays/displaySections (Title / Header / Body / Footnote), referencedAnalyses (empty v0.1; reserved mintverse handoff), x-tabular (rendering geometry, pagination, style trace, input provenance). Determinism contract: two consecutive emit() calls byte- identical except rendered_at parameter timestamp; YAML round-trips yaml::read_yaml() + yaml::write_yaml(). Pure dispatcher. emit() rendering ; composes as_grid() backend writer. inspect resolved grid without writing file (development, build custom downstream consumer), call as_grid() directly.

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
code:
# ---- Example 1: Render demographics to Markdown ---- # # Smallest possible emit: spec in, .md out. The backend is chosen # from the file extension; the engine pipeline runs internally, # then the registered md backend writes a GFM pipe table you can # preview in any Markdown renderer. tempfile() keeps the example # clean for `R CMD check`. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  demo <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Source: ADSL." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))  demo_md <- tempfile(fileext = ".md") emit(demo, demo_md)  # ---- Example 2: Render + QC data + CDISC audit manifest ---- # # The clinical double-programming pattern: render the table, # write a QC CSV alongside it for an independent programmer to # verify cell-for-cell, and emit the CDISC ARS audit manifest # for submission packaging. The lambda derives the QC path from # the render path so the sponsor's naming convention lives in one # place.  ae_spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_aesocpt,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))  ae_md <- tempfile(fileext = ".md") emit(   ae_spec,   ae_md,   data_file = \(f) paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f), "_qc.csv"),   manifest  = TRUE )  # ---- Example 3: Same spec, four backends — one-loop fan-out ---- # # `emit()` dispatches by file extension, so the same spec can # render to every backend in one loop. Useful for visual diffs # across formats during development and for shipping a build # artefact set (RTF for submission, HTML for review, PDF for the # CSR appendix). eff_spec <- tabular(cdisc_eff_resp, titles = "Best Overall Response") |>   cols(     stat_label  = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Response"),     row_type    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     groupid     = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     group_label = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo     = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50     = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100    = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal")   )  out_dir <- tempfile() dir.create(out_dir) for (ext in c(".html", ".rtf", ".tex", ".docx", ".md")) {   emit(eff_spec, file.path(out_dir, paste0("eff", ext))) } list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "eff.docx" "eff.html" "eff.md"   "eff.rtf"  "eff.tex"   # ---- Example 4: QC artefact via data_file alongside the render ---- # # `emit(data_file = ...)` writes the resolved post-engine wide # data frame alongside the rendered table. The sponsor's QC # programmer picks up the side-car .csv (or .rds) and validates # cell values without parsing the rendered RTF. rtf_out  <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf") data_out <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") emit(eff_spec, rtf_out, data_file = data_out) file.exists(rtf_out) #> [1] TRUE file.exists(data_out) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 5: Render into a not-yet-existing output folder ---- # # `create_dir = TRUE` builds the destination directory tree on the # fly, so a submission-folder layout can be written in one pass # without a separate `dir.create()` step. nested <- file.path(tempfile(), "tables", "safety", "eff.md") emit(eff_spec, nested, create_dir = TRUE) file.exists(nested) #> [1] TRUE

title: Wrap a plot or image in submission chrome — figure
text: Builds figure display, "F" TFL. figure() takes ggplot, recorded base-R plot, zero-argument drawing function, path PNG / JPG file, surrounds canonical submission chrome table: four centred title lines, footnotes, per-page header / footer drawn active preset(). Pass list emit one figure per page single file.

title: Wrap a plot or image in submission chrome — figure
code: figure(   plot,   titles = NULL,   footnotes = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   halign = "center",   valign = "middle",   dpi = 300,   meta = NULL )

title: Wrap a plot or image in submission chrome — figure
text: plot figure display. One : ggplot object; recorded base plot grDevices::recordPlot(); zero-argument function draws active device called; length-1 path .png, .jpg, .jpeg file; list multi-page figure. Tip: list may mix kinds freely — ggplot, recorded plot, PNG path can share one multi-page figure. titles Title lines figure. <character> | NULL. One element per line, four; {glue} interpolation md() / html() inline formatting tabular(). NULL draws titles. footnotes Footnote lines figure. <character> | NULL. One element per line; interpolation inline formatting titles. NULL draws footnotes. width Drawn image width inches. <numeric(1)> | NULL. NULL fills full printable width. height Drawn image height inches. <numeric(1)> | NULL. NULL fills body box height (printable height minus title footnote chrome). Set smaller value leave vertical slack valign place image within. halign Horizontal placement content box. <character(1)>. One : "left" "center" (default) "right" valign Vertical placement content box. <character(1)>. One : "top" "middle" (default) "bottom" Note: continuous backends (HTML / Markdown) render figure contained viewport fixed page height, valign -op ; paged backends honour exactly. dpi Raster resolution plot inputs. <numeric(1)>. Resolution dots per inch PNG rasterisation. Ignored file inputs (passed unchanged) vector PDF targets. meta Per-page token data frame. <data.frame> | NULL. Multi-page : one row per plot, whose columns become {token} values page's titles / footnotes. Ignored (warning) single-plot figure.

title: Wrap a plot or image in submission chrome — figure
text: figure_spec. Pass emit() write file, print preview figure inline.

title: Wrap a plot or image in submission chrome — figure
text: Two-axis placement. default image FILLS body content box — full printable width box height (printable height minus title / footnote chrome). Pass explicit width / height smaller box halign / valign place image within resulting slack, independently — horizontally (left / center / right) vertically (top / middle / bottom), defaulting centred. Paged backends (RTF / PDF / DOCX) honour valign exactly content-box height; box-filling default vertical slack, valign bites set shorter height. continuous backends (HTML / Markdown) render figure responsively, contained viewport, halign still applies valign -op . Format-aware rasterisation. Plot inputs render vector PDF .pdf / .tex targets PNG dpi every backend; file inputs pass byte--byte. raster work happens emit(). Styling chrome. figure carries chrome surfaces table, style() preset() cosmetic knobs reach titles, footnotes, page header / footer — e.g. style(fig, font_size = 14, .= cells_title()) preset(fig, colors = list(footnotes = c(text = "grey40"))). figure body, column headers, subgroup banner, styling surfaces error. Inter-section spacing follows preset spacing knob (title, footnote), exactly like table.

title: Attach an auto-numbered footnote to a table location — footnote
text: Anchor footnote cell, column header, title line, cells_*() location. engine assigns marker, places superscript every matching anchor, emits marked-footnote line foot table. Markers assigned , reading order, deduped id, byte-identical across every backend (RTF / LaTeX / PDF / HTML / DOCX) every page, marker anchor can never desynchronise note.

title: Attach an auto-numbered footnote to a table location — footnote
code: footnote(.spec, text, .at = cells_body(), id = NULL, symbol = NULL)

title: Attach an auto-numbered footnote to a table location — footnote
text: .spec tabular_spec annotate. <tabular_spec>: required. text footnote text. <character(1)> | md() | html(). Wrap md() / html() inline markup; plain strings shown verbatim. plain string supports glue-style {expr} interpolation, evaluated R code calling environment build time (double brace literal one); md() / html() value passed without interpolation. .marker placed. <tabular_location>: default [cells_body()]. cells_*() location: body-cell predicate (cells_body(= ...)), column header (cells_headers()), title line (cells_title()), .   Note: styling argument ., never . id Stable identifier sharing one marker across anchors. <character(1)> | NULL. Two footnote() calls id share single marker single note line. NULL (default) makes call note. symbol Pin explicit marker glyph. <character(1)> | NULL. Overrides auto-allocated marker note (e.g. "*"). pinned symbol reserved skipped auto-allocator, never collides. NULL (default) auto-allocates preset scheme.
code: # data-driven body anchor: mark every high-frequency preferred term footnote(spec, "Includes events of any severity.",          .at = cells_body(where = n_total >= 50, j = "label"))  # column-header anchor: mark the analysis-population denominator footnote(spec, "Safety population.",          .at = cells_headers(j = "Total"))

title: Attach an auto-numbered footnote to a table location — footnote

title: Attach an auto-numbered footnote to a table location — footnote
text: Engine-assigned, never hand-typed. Unlike literal ^^ typed cell footnotes argument, footnote() marker allocated resolve engine decimal alignment, never disturbs column alignment never drifts sync. scheme (letters / numbers / symbols) block-line format come active preset (footnote_markers, footnote_label). Dedup id. Give two anchors id share one marker one note line. Without id, footnote() call note. Coexists footnotes. Manual footnotes lines render first; auto-numbered block follows. two systems cross-dedup, mix hand-typed marker engine one note.

what: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
text: Return preset_spec last attached via set_preset(), NULL session default set. cascade resolver calls internally; users call diagnostics ("session inheriting?") copy active default per-spec override via preset().

title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
code: get_preset()

title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
text: preset_spec, NULL session default active.

title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
code: # ---- Example 1: Inspect after setting a session default ---- # # `get_preset()` returns NULL before any session default has been # attached, then returns the `preset_spec` after `set_preset()`. get_preset()  # NULL #> NULL  set_preset(font_size = 8, orientation = "landscape")  active <- get_preset() is_preset_spec(active)     # TRUE #> [1] TRUE active@font_size            # 8 #> [1] 8 active@orientation          # "landscape" #> [1] "landscape"  # ---- Example 2: Copy the session default into a per-spec override ---- # # Read the session preset, tweak one knob for a single table, and # attach as a per-spec override without disturbing the session. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter")  # Read-tweak-attach without mutating the session default. base_knobs <- get_preset() tabular(cdisc_saf_n) |>   preset(     font_size   = base_knobs@font_size,     paper_size  = base_knobs@paper_size,     orientation = "landscape"   )  #tabular-f3a8112265 { font-family: "Courier New", Courier, "Liberation Mono", monospace; title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Build column-header band(s) rendered table. named argument one band; value either character vector column names (leaf band) named list bands (inner band). Nesting depth arbitrary — engine renders one band row per depth level, cell spanning columns leaves.

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
code: headers(.spec, ...)

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: .spec tabular_spec attach header tree . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short user-supplied band label ... spec slot. ... Named header bands. name band label (must non-blank); value either: character vector data-column names — leaf band, named list whose entries follow recursive pattern — inner band. Inside nested-list value, unnamed character-vector entry declares passthrough leaf (see Passthrough section ). Restriction: Every column referenced must exist .spec@data. column may appear one leaf. Names must unique within one headers() call. Tip: Pass headers() arguments clear tree. Interaction: Band labels support glue-style {expr} interpolation, evaluated R code calling environment build time (double brace literal one). non-blank uniqueness checks apply raw author-typed name, interpolation.

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining sort_rows(), style().

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Replace, stack. second headers() call REPLACES prior tree — header structure single spec, stackable list. Call arguments clear tree. Strict label rule. Every declared band label must carry visible text — empty strings, NA, whitespace-labels rejected every nesting level. stricter col_spec(), accept empty labels (row-label column header text legitimate clinical case). silently-blank band layout artefact. Uncovered columns render naked. Columns referenced band render col_spec.label — extra band row . canonical pattern row-label columns (variable, soc, stat_label). Multi-line band labels. Embed \n band label two-line band cell (arm name row 1, BigN row 2). Spanner underline trim (backend limitation). spanner's underline trimmed ends, booktabs \cmidrule(lr) style, adjacent spanners separated visible gap rather merging one continuous line. PDF / LaTeX (tabularray leftpos/rightpos) HTML (inset rule) render trim natively. RTF DOCX inset cell border horizontally, spanner underline spans full band width (adjacent spanner rules abut). known, documented limitation OOXML / RTF cell-border model, bug.

what: Passthrough leaves inside a nested band
title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Inside nested-list value, child entry may unnamed — entry character vector column names sit directly parent intermediate band depth. Use one column band sub-grouping siblings . strict-label rule still applies every declared band; unnamed passthrough band missing label — "band declared depth column."

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events System Organ Class Preferred Term Safety Population   Subjects counted per SOC per PT.   Table 14.2.1 Best Overall Response Response Rates Efficacy Evaluable Population   Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment.   Table 14.4.1 Vital Signs Summary     Demographics, hierarchical headers

The # row-label column (`soc`) sits to the left of the band with no # header covering it — the canonical clinical layout. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  tabular(   cdisc_saf_aesocpt,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50

N={n['Total']}")   ) |>   headers(     "Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))  #tabular-fcbe777680 Backends translate CSS keys destination-specific markup (RTF \cf, LaTeX \textcolor, DOCX , HTML inline style). Backend-specific raw codes. span data-rtf, data-latex, data-html, data-docx attributes carries per-backend raw markup. matching backend emits data value verbatim ignores others; non-matching backends render span's text content plain. Use cases AST express portably.

what: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: Wrap length-1 character vector tabular(), col_spec(), style() pretext / posttext, similar string slots interpret CommonMark Markdown render time. Supports GitHub-flavoured plus Pandoc-style superscript (^sup^) subscript (~sub~) extensions; raw HTML inside Markdown passes constrained tag set documented html().

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
code: md(text)

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: text Markdown string. <character(1)>: required. Length-1 character vector. NA rejected; empty string "" renders content.

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: length-1 character vector classed c("from_markdown", "character"). Pass directly string-bearing slot (tabular() titles / footnotes, col_spec() label, style() pretext / posttext); resolve engine calls .parse_inline() internally backends walk resulting inline_ast.

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: Convention adopted gt. Marking strings md() html() mirrors well-tested gt convention. Plain (unwrapped) strings render plain text — stray ** silently bold surrounding span. Wrap explicitly opt . Recognised Markdown. **bold**, *italic*, `code`, [link text](url), hard line break (two trailing spaces + \ + ), Pandoc ^sup^ ~sub~. Single embedded ("soft break" CommonMark) renders space HTML; tabular preserves line break clinical-table use multi-line cells / titles routine. HTML pass-. Raw HTML Markdown (e.g. md("Drug warning")) parsed HTML using tag whitelist html(). Tags outside whitelist drop wrapper keep text content. Composition plain strings. md() html() wrap input internal control-character prefix survives c() concatenation, can freely mix plain marked strings single character vector: c("Table 14.3.1", md("**Drug **"), "third"). Backends strip marker rendering; users never see .

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: Attach pagination_spec tabular_spec. engine uses spec render time decide page breaks fall, wide tables split horizontal panels, continuation marker () prints continued pages. row budget per page computed engine active preset (paper, orientation, margins, font size) chrome rows consumed titles, column headers, footnotes — set rows-per-page directly.

title: Configure pagination — paginate
code: paginate(   .spec,   keep_together = character(),   panels = 1,   orphan_floor = 3,   widow_floor = 2,   repeat_content = c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"),   continuation = NULL )

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: .spec tabular_spec attach pagination . <tabular_spec>: required. keep_together Group columns whose runs identical values must split across page break. <character>: default character(). Every entry must usage = "group" column declared cols(). Interaction: run tall fit computed row budget less orphan_floor split anyway; pagination best-effort, hard contract.   panels Number horizontal panels wide tables. <integer(1)>: default 1. 1, every column every page (single vertical scroll). N > 1, engine splits non-group columns N chunks repeats every group column every panel. orphan_floor Minimum rows continued-page. <integer(1)>: default 3. keep_together move page break back far fewer orphan_floor rows ride current page, engine splits protected run anyway. Acts escape valve groups tall fit. widow_floor Minimum rows final page. <integer(1)>: default 2. last page carry fewer widow_floor rows, engine merges rows back onto previous page (page overflow accepted). Avoids "one-row-orphaned--page-N" look without complicating primary split rule. repeat_content page chrome repeats every page. <character>: default c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"). subset three values; governed independently: "titles" — title block every page (else page 1 ). "headers" — column-header band every page (else page 1 ). "footnotes" — footnote block every page (else last page ). default repeats three page self-contained per submission layout contract. Pass subset drop one (e.g. c("headers", "footnotes") keeps title page 1 ), character() repeat nothing. Note: Footnotes always anchored page foot present; membership chooses every-page vs last-page-, never table-body placement. HTML / MD: ignored. HTML renders one continuous <table> browsers natively repeat <thead> print; MD print model. Effective page-oriented backends (RTF, PDF, LaTeX, DOCX). continuation Marker text appended continuing table's title block. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. NULL (default) renders marker — pick wording submission style guide expects (e.g. "(continued)", "(Cont'd)", "Page %d %d") pass explicitly. Backend support uneven — verify render target: PDF / LaTeX — full: marker prints every continuation page (vertical page overflow horizontal panels). RTF — horizontal continuation panels (paginate(panels = N)); marker appear vertical page-overflow continuations. DOCX — marked. DOCX paginates natively emits continuation marker. HTML / MD — ignored. one continuous document screen continuing-page boundary mark.
code: # Protect the SOC-level grouping in an AE-by-SOC/PT table. paginate(keep_together = "soc")

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining style(), preset(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: Replace, stack. second paginate() call REPLACES prior spec — pagination single configuration block, stackable list. Call defaults clear back engine's auto behaviour. Rows per page computed, configured. engine takes paper height active orientation (letter, a4) subtracts top + bottom margins, title block height (number title lines + blank separator), column-header band height (max embedded \n line count across visible column labels, plus spanning header levels), footnote block height (number footnote lines + blank separator). remainder, divided row height active font size, gives body-row budget per page. Landscape pages naturally carry fewer rows portrait paper size; smaller fonts carry . keep_together protects group runs. page break fall middle contiguous run identical values usage = "group" column listed keep_together, engine moves break BACK start run whole run rides next page. Single rule escape: moving break back leave fewer orphan_floor rows current page, engine splits run anyway (single group tall fit one page kept together). panels group stickiness. panels > 1, engine splits NON-group columns approximately equal slices repeats every usage = "group" column every panel row context.

what: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: pivot_across() tabular's input-side helper: consumes long Analysis Results Data (ARD) data frame (typically produced cards::ard_stack() cards::ard_stack_hierarchical()) returns wide display data.frame ready pass tabular().

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
code: pivot_across(   data,   statistic = list(continuous = "{mean} ({sd})", categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"),   column = NULL,   row_group = NULL,   label = NULL,   overall = "Total",   decimals = NULL,   fmt = NULL,   aux = NULL )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: data Long ARD input data. <data.frame>: required. minimum needs stat_name stat. Cards-style group columns (group1, group1_level, ...) variable / variable_level auto-detected. Tibbles / card objects / arrow tables coerced via .data.frame(). statistic Format spec cell composition. <character(1) | named list>: required. Combines one ARD stats one display cell. Three accepted forms — illustrated . Inside format string, {stat_name} substitutes stat's value ARD (example, "{n} ({p}%)" interpolates n p stats "53 (62%)" cell). lookup order value needed variable : per-variable -> per-context -> default -> literal "{n}". column runs across top table. <character | NULL>: default NULL. single grouping variable whose unique values become arm columns (NULL auto-detects group1, picks single non-standard column renamed input). Two reserved tokens turn analysis variable column band: c(".variable", "<arm>") — variable becomes band arm columns; statistic entries stack rows. cells combined strings (e.g. "323.9 (106)"). Emitted columns named "<variable>..<arm>". c(".variable", ".stat") — variable becomes band whose statistic entries become columns (landscape "value change" shell); arm drops leading row stub. Emitted columns named "<variable>..<stat-entry>". Anything present ARD named column (row_group) stacks rows. Per-variable statistic / decimals resolve inside band, bands may carry different (even different-length) stat lists; ragged bands pad NA. Tip: reference emitted column names verbatim manual headers() call build band spanners; pivot_across build spanners . row_group Second, non-column grouping dimension. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. Names non-arm group variable two-variable .(e.g. SEX ard_stack(.= c(ARM, SEX))). widens leading row column (pivoted arm column), result composes subgroup(= ...) col_spec(usage = "group") downstream. required. cards encodes crossing factor SOC/PT hierarchy identically (second group variable appears variable -marginal rows), two told apart automatically. Naming row_group declares "crossing factor": -marginal rows dropped flat path used. Leave NULL genuine hierarchy. Restriction: Must name second grouping variable present ARD must differ column. label Variable-name display-label map. <character> | NULL: default NULL. Named character vector mapping variable names display labels (e.g. c(AGE = "Age (years)", SEX = "Sex")). Applies variable, soc, label columns output. NULL leaves upstream variable names verbatim. Renaming hierarchical "overall" row. cards::ard_stack_hierarchical(overall = TRUE) ARD carries internal ..ard_hierarchical_overall.. sentinel grand-total ("event") row. relabelled "Overall" default; map sentinel key override, e.g. label = c("..ard_hierarchical_overall.." = "TOTAL SUBJECTS EVENT"). raw sentinel never reaches output hierarchy depth. overall Column name NA-arm (overall / total) rows. <character(1) | NULL>: default "Total". Pass NULL drop overall rows entirely (per-arm output). decimals Per-stat decimal precision. <named integer | named list>: default c()“. Accepts three forms: named integer vector — global per-stat overrides (c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 0)). named list keyed variable — per-variable plus .default (list(AGE = c(mean = 2), .default = c(p = 1))). named list keyed row_group value — per-group precision one call (list(SYSBP = c(mean = 0, sd = 1), WEIGHT = c(mean = 1), .default = c(mean = 1, sd = 2))). entry per-token spec (named numeric vector, bare scalar applied every token). Built-defaults apply none sets stat. Interaction: list decimals read per-row_group row_group set every key (apart .default) one levels; otherwise stays per-variable. Within matched group token falls back group token, per-group .default token, built-default. group present data absent list (NA / ungrouped rows) uses .default. row_group NULL keys match variable, pivot_across() errors asks row_group. fmt Per-stat custom formatter functions. <named list function>: default list()“. function takes numeric value returns character string; overrides built-ins decimals stat. Useful p-value styling domain-specific formatting. aux Auxiliary comparison columns second ARD. <named list | NULL>: default NULL. entry -arm statistic (difference, hazard ratio, p-value, ...) computed ARD bound trailing column, aligned 1:1 row_group key. entry name column name; reference manual headers() call. entry list: ard — auxiliary ARD (required), one row per row_group. statistic — format string (e.g. "{estimate} ({conf.low}, {conf.high})"); defaults "{n}". decimals / fmt — optional, main pivot. Entries append left right. One entry one column; several comparison columns (estimate p-value) pass several entries. Requirement: needs row_group; auxiliary rows align main table , must unique 1:1 sides (many--many key silently fabricate rows, aborts).
code: # p-value formatter: render below-threshold values as "<0.001". fmt = list(   p.value = function(x) {     ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", x))   } )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: One format string applied every variable regardless context. Use ARD homogeneous (e.g. categorical).
code: # Every variable rendered as "n (p%)" — categorical-only slice. cat_only <- cdisc_saf_demo_ard[cdisc_saf_demo_ard$context == "categorical", ] pivot_across(   cat_only,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)" )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: Different formats per context. typical clinical-table form demographics mix continuous categorical variables. list names must match values ARD's context column verbatim. strings appear depends ARD built: cards::ard_continuous() / ard_categorical() emit "continuous" / "categorical". cards::ard_summary() / ard_tabulate() emit "summary" / "tabulate". ARD assembled ard_stack(ard_summary(...), ard_tabulate(...)) keyed summary / tabulate, continuous / categorical. Inspect unique(ard$context) unsure.
code: # AGE (continuous) -> "75.2 (8.59)"; SEX (categorical) -> "53 (62%)" pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous  = "{mean} ({sd})",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: Override per-variable basis; fall back default context. Use one variable needs custom format.
code: # AGE shows just the mean; SEX / RACE keep the categorical default. pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     AGE         = "{mean}",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)",     default     = "{mean} ({sd})"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: single entry can named character vector — element becomes one display row, name row label. Use N / Mean (SD) / Median / Min, Max-style blocks.
code: pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous = c(       N           = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",       Median      = "{median}",       "Min, Max"  = "{min}, {max}"     ),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: wide data.frame ready tabular(). Schema: variable — variable name (label label = ...). stat_label — display-row label. One column per arm level (named group1_level values renamed arm column). Total (whatever overall set ) applicable. leading column named row_group set (second grouping dimension). Hierarchical ARD adds soc, label, row_type instead variable. Pass result straight tabular() start render pipeline.

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: tabular's package boundary display-: pre-summarised data , rendered file . pivot_across() canonical bridge cards aggregation backend boundary. aggregate — pivots arms columns, interpolates per-cell display strings stat values, applies decimal precision. Filtering, weighting, aggregation happen upstream cards data-prep step.

what: Key statistic by the ARD context
title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: statistic (fmt) matched ARD's context column verbatim, value differs per generating function. Keying wrong name silently drops format. Inspect unique(ard$context) first key match (pass single format string / default = cover everything). explicitly-supplied statistic matches context , pivot_across() warns rather silently emitting {n}.

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: row whose n value equals zero renders whole cell bare n value instead fully interpolating format string. categorical level n = 0, cell shows "0", "0 (0.0%)". clinical convention — empty cells read single zero, advertise meaningless rate. default fires (chain events). cell assembly, format-string interpolation, engine checks row's n stat. zero, engine short-circuits returns formatted n value ("0") entire cell — {p} never substituted, (0.0%) half format string dropped. opt : supply custom fmt$n. Setting function fmt$n engine's signal user owns n rendering. short-circuit disabled whole table; every row full format string interpolates, {n} becomes formatter's output {p} becomes standard percentage. n = 0, "0 (0.0%)".
code: # Force "0 (0.0%)" for n = 0 rows by attaching a custom n formatter. # The body of fmt$n can be the default integer rendering — its # presence alone is what disables the zero-suppression branch. pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous  = "{mean} ({sd})",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   fmt = list(n = function(x) sprintf("%d", as.integer(x))) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: percentage otherwise round 0 (value positive smaller chosen precision) renders <0.1; one round 100 (positive smaller 100) renders >99.9. threshold precision-aware: decimals = c(p = 2) produces <0.01 / >99.99. matches pharma convention never claiming exactly 0% 100% least one subject contributed. Override per-stat via fmt:   fmt$p receives raw stat value (proportion 0 1) returns displayed string. pharma-threshold branch fires inside built-p formatter decimals-driven path, custom fmt$p bypasses .
code: # Show exact rounded percentages even at the extremes pivot_across(   data,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)",   decimals  = c(p = 1),   fmt = list(p = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x * 100)) )
