line-height: 0; font-size: 0; } #tabular-a25cab255c .tabular-table + .tabular-table { page-break-before: always; break-before: page; } }   Table 14.2.1 Summary of Continuous Parameters by Treatment Safety Population    AgeWeightStatisticAGE..PlaceboAGE..Xanomeline High DoseAGE..Xanomeline Low DoseWEIGHT..PlaceboWEIGHT..Xanomeline High DoseWEIGHT..Xanomeline Low DoseN867296867295Mean (SD)75.2 (8.59)73.8 (7.94)76.0 (8.11)62.8 (12.77)69.5 (14.35)68.0 (14.50)Median76.075.578.060.669.066.7  # ---- Example 7: Statistics as columns within each band ---- # # `column = c(".variable", ".stat")` spreads each statistic entry into # its own column (the landscape shell); the arm drops to a leading row # stub. Emitted columns are named "<variable>..<stat-entry>". Bands may # carry different stat sets, with no row-alignment needed. vitals |>   pivot_across(     column = c(".variable", ".stat"),     overall = NULL,     statistic = list(       AGE    = c(N = "{N}", Mean = "{mean}", SD = "{sd}"),       WEIGHT = c(N = "{N}", Mean = "{mean}", SD = "{sd}", Median = "{median}")     ),     decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, median = 1)   ) #>                 TRT01A AGE..N AGE..Mean AGE..SD WEIGHT..N WEIGHT..Mean #> 1              Placebo     86      75.2    8.59        86         62.8 #> 2 Xanomeline High Dose     72      73.8    7.94        72         69.5 #> 3  Xanomeline Low Dose     96      76.0    8.11        95         68.0 #>   WEIGHT..SD WEIGHT..Median #> 1      12.77           60.6 #> 2      14.35           69.0 #> 3      14.50           66.7  # ---- Example 8: Per-row-group decimal precision ---- # # A by-parameter vitals table where each parameter carries its own # value precision: systolic BP to 0 dp, weight to 1 dp, in ONE call. # `decimals` is keyed by the `row_group` (PARAM) value; the engine # selects each row's token precision by its parameter. No bundled ARD # carries a second grouping dimension, so build a tiny one inline. vital_ard <- do.call(rbind, lapply(   list(     c("SYSBP", "Placebo", "mean", "133.27"),     c("SYSBP", "Placebo", "sd", "15.81"),     c("SYSBP", "Drug", "mean", "128.94"),     c("SYSBP", "Drug", "sd", "14.02"),     c("WEIGHT", "Placebo", "mean", "71.43"),     c("WEIGHT", "Placebo", "sd", "12.77"),     c("WEIGHT", "Drug", "mean", "73.06"),     c("WEIGHT", "Drug", "sd", "13.19")   ),   function(r) {     data.frame(       group1 = "PARAM",       group1_level = r[[1]],       group2 = "TRTA",       group2_level = r[[2]],       variable = "AVAL",       variable_level = NA_character_,       context = "continuous",       stat_name = r[[3]],       stat_label = r[[3]],       stat = I(list(as.numeric(r[[4]]))),       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   } )) vital_ard |>   pivot_across(     column = "TRTA",     row_group = "PARAM",     overall = NULL,     statistic = list(continuous = "{mean} ({sd})"),     decimals = list(       SYSBP = c(mean = 0, sd = 1),       WEIGHT = c(mean = 1, sd = 2)     )   ) #>    PARAM variable stat_label      Placebo         Drug #> 1  SYSBP     AVAL       AVAL   133 (15.8)   129 (14.0) #> 2 WEIGHT     AVAL       AVAL 71.4 (12.77) 73.1 (13.19)

what: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Attach preset_spec tabular_spec, carrying page-geometry knobs (paper, orientation, margins, body font_size + family, h-rule policy, decimal metric, typography defaults). engine consults per-spec preset first computing per-page row budget, decimal-aligned column widths, chrome backend renders around body grid.

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
code: preset(.spec, ..., .template = NULL, .style = NULL, .reset = FALSE)

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: .spec spec attach preset . <tabular_spec | figure_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind knob name ... spec slot. Note: figure() spec accepts page-geometry knobs (paper_size, orientation, margins, font_size, font_family, pagehead, pagefoot, ...) plus cosmetic surface knobs (alignment / fonts / colors / padding) target chrome surfaces, titles footnotes, e.g. fonts = list(titles = c(size = 14)). cosmetic knob targets table-surface (body / header / subgroup), rules knob (rules sit header band figure lacks), .template / .style style templates rejected, since figure surfaces. ... Named preset knobs. subset preset knobs preset_spec class carries. Knob values validated class's enum / length / type rules; bad values raise tabular_error_input. Unknown knob names raise tabular_error_input recognised set listed. Recognised knobs: font_size — body point size. <numeric(1)>. font_family — body font family. <character | character(1)>. Default "mono". Four accepted shapes: Generic family — "mono" (default), "serif", "sans" (CSS aliases "monospace" / "sans-serif" also recognised). resolver expands per-backend chain leads Microsoft Office face (Courier New / Times New Roman / Arial) — installed every Windows macOS machine, documents reviewed — PostScript legacy name, metric-compatible Liberation face LAST Linux-server fallback (Posit Workbench / Domino / Citrix / RStudio Server), TeX Gyre LaTeX compile / CSS generic HTML. Liberation Mono / Serif / Sans metric-compatible Courier New / TNR / Arial, layout, line breaks, decimal alignment hold across every render context regardless end chain resolves. mono default matches dominant submission-TFL convention deterministic glyph widths drive n (%) cell alignment. Named alias — "Times", "Times New Roman", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Courier", "Courier New". PostScript-era names alias appropriate generic family (Times -> serif, Arial / Helvetica -> sans, Courier -> mono) emit expanded chain. Honours user's intent ("want Times-like rendering") every OS instead hard-erroring Linux server TNR installed. Arbitrary named font — "Inter", "JetBrains Mono", "IBM Plex Mono", "Source Code Pro", sponsor-specific face, etc. Emitted verbatim fallback fabricated. consuming app (browser, xelatex, Word, LibreOffice) resolves name font matcher: RTF DOCX substitute name missing; xelatex hard-errors compile time face installed; HTML browsers fall browser's default font (necessarily class-matched). portable result, prefer generic family (shape 1) explicit stack (shape 4). Explicit stack — c("Inter", "Helvetica", "sans"). User owns chain. Returned verbatim — alias lookup bypassed, c("Times", "Times") honours exact name chain expansion (escape hatch users genuinely want exact-name semantics). Note: tabular bundles fonts. default chain leads Office face, Word's font dropdown shows face reader actually installed Windows / macOS (e.g. Courier New "mono"), phantom name; metric-compatible Liberation face appears fallback headless Linux box. orientation — page orientation. <character(1)>. One "landscape" (default), "portrait". paper_size — paper key. <character(1)>. One "letter" (default), "a4". margins — page margins inches. <numeric(1) | numeric(4)>. Length 1 = four sides; length 4 = top, right, bottom, left. pagehead, pagefoot — per-page header / footer band content. <list>. band named list slots left / center / right; every slot name rejected. slot accepts NULL (omit), character scalar, character vector (multi-row content), inline_ast. Empty list() (default) -> band emitted. Single-row form (scalar slots):   Multi-row form (vector slots, index-aligned):   Growth direction. Vector index 1 = body edge; index N = far body. pagehead rows stack upward away table (row closest table index 1). pagefoot rows stack downward away table (row closest table index 1). Shorter slots pad "" FAR end (high index), scalar slot naturally lands body-edge row. Token vocabulary — substituted slot text: {program}, {program_path}, {datetime} resolve per render (as_grid() / emit()); {page} {npages} resolve per page (filled Word / xelatex / browser's print engine view time). program tokens walk 5-mode detection chain — RStudio API, source() frame, Rscript / R CMD BATCH commandArgs (covers Domino + Linux batch + CI), knitr current_input, fallback "<interactive>". rules — single border vocabulary (replaces old borders knob). String sugar "booktabs" (default, clinical baseline), "grid", "frame", "none"; single brdr() broadcast every active rule; named list keyed nine rule names (toprule, midrule, bottomrule, spanrule, rowrule, footnoterule, leftrule, rightrule, colrule) — unlisted rules keep default, bare string "none" drops one. rules = list(rowrule = brdr()) reproduces old hlines = "". bottomrule vs footnoterule. mutually exclusive: exactly one rule sits data -> footnote boundary. default bottomrule (table's bottom edge); footnoterule (table-width rule opening footnote section) default. distinct footnote-section rule, footnoterule drawn paginated backends — RTF, LaTeX / PDF, DOCX. HTML backend continuous (non-paginated) separate footnote section, folds one rule: whichever bottomrule / footnoterule active becomes table's bottom edge (bottomrule wins set). Setting footnoterule therefore still draws closing rule HTML — rendered bottomrule. spacing — region-keyed blank-line control. named list keyed title / body / subgroup / footnote, named numeric c(= , = ) (footnote: ). Default one blank line title block. Two adjoining region-sides target physical gap resolve MAX (never sum), gap never accidentally doubled. stripe — zebra body-row fills. single colour (applied even rows) named c(odd = , even = ); NULL (default) . indent_size — row-label indent width, monospace- space units. <integer(1)>. Default 2L. indent level adds many space-widths left padding cell. 0L disables indent prefix entirely. Backends native padding-left semantics (HTML / LaTeX / RTF / DOCX / PDF) emit cell padding wrapped continuation lines align indented baseline; Markdown carries literal space-prefix. Block alignment title / footnote / header / subgroup / body surfaces set via alignment named-list knob (alignment = list(title_halign = "left", ...)), scalar knob; blank-line spacing set via spacing (). na_text — global NA fallback. <character(1)>. decimal_metrics — decimal-padding metric. <character(1)>. "afm" (default) measures glyphs bundled Core font metrics, decimal columns align width-exact proportional fonts (within one padding space rounding; exact Courier). "chars" pads character count — exact monospaced faces . Markdown output always pads character count, correct geometry text medium. decimal_markers — missing-value tokens recognised col_spec(align = "decimal"). <character>. Default c("NR", "NE", "NC", "ND", "BLQ"). cell whose trimmed value one treated non-numeric marker: shown right-aligned column rather parsed number, marker appearing inside compound (e.g. upper bound 14.3 (11.2, NR)) preserved slot-aligned. Excludes "-", "NA", "INF"/"-INF" default: "-" collides range separators, "NA" handled na_text, infinities real values. Set character(0) disable marker handling. width_mode — table-level column-sizing policy. Mirrors Word's Table Layout menu. <character(1)>. One : "content" (default) — column auto-sized max(body, header). table fill page. Word's "Auto-fit Contents". "window" — Auto-sized columns scaled proportionally natural width fill residual page width. Pinned percent columns keep pins. Word's "Auto-fit Window". "fixed" — explicit per-column widths drive layout. Auto-sized columns collapse minimum sliver. Word's "Fixed Column Width". Interaction: Pair col_spec(width = ...) pins drive layout "window" / "fixed". "content", pins still take priority auto columns. HTML backend. width_mode drives paper backends (LaTeX / RTF / PDF / DOCX) . HTML unconditionally responsive — table always fills parent columns wrap viewport narrows, regardless width_mode. Per-column widths (col_spec(width)) emit verbatim HTML colgroup per gt convention. empty_text — house-style wording empty-state message shown spec resolves zero data rows. <character(1)>. resolution spec arg -> preset knob -> built-default: per-table tabular(empty_text = ...) wins, else preset knob (set via set_preset() whole house style), else built-"data available report". Glue {} md() / html() inline formatting honoured, exactly like title line. whitespace — significant ASCII spaces labels cells render. <character(1)>. One : "preserve" (default) — leading, trailing, interior runs 2+ spaces become backend non-breaking token (  / ~ / \~; DOCX preserves via xml:space), hand-built indent like col_spec(label = "    Placebo") renders verbatim across every backend. single interior space stays breakable, cells still wrap. "collapse" — leave backend's native run-folding place (HTML / md / LaTeX collapse runs one space). Note: never affects col_spec(align = "decimal") padding, uses U+00A0 preserved unconditionally. chrome_onscreen — whether -screen running header / footer bands render HTML output. <character(1)>. One : "auto" (default) — pagehead / pagefoot content renders -screen <header> / <footer> bands. "" — suppress -screen bands; print-time @page chrome still renders, print--PDF output unchanged. Useful HTML consumed via print. HTML-; paged backends (RTF / PDF / DOCX) always emit per-page chrome regardless knob. footnote_markers — glyph scheme footnote() markers, engine allocates reading order. <character(1)>. One : "letters" (default) — , b, …, z, aa, ab, … (bijective base-26). "numbers" — 1, 2, 3, … "symbols" — Lamport's sequence *, †, ‡, §, ¶, ‖, doubled (**, ††, …) spills past sixth. Interaction: note's anchor fixed footnote(); scheme (knob) label (footnote_label) resolved active preset render, flipping either re-letters every marker . footnote_label — block-line template footnote() marker. <character(1)>. Default "{m}"; {m} token replaced allocated marker, "[{m}]" prints [] ahead note text footnote line. cell_padding — cell padding points, CSS shorthand length 1 / 2 / 4 (| vertical horizontal | top right bottom left), parsed length rule margins. <numeric>: default c(0, 5.4) (vertical 0, horizontal 5.4pt). single source truth auto column-width measurement (left + right) every backend's cell margin, measured rendered widths agree. Interaction: body per-side padding override (preset(padding = list(body = ...)) style(.= cells_body(), padding = ...)) takes precedence measurement render. Note: DOCX LaTeX render left / right exactly; RTF (\\trgaph one symmetric gap) renders average, total width still matches two sides look equal.   .template preset_spec bulk-apply .... <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. supplied, every knob template set away factory default feeds base layer; user-supplied ... knobs merge top. List-valued knobs (rules, fonts, colors, padding, alignment) shallow-merge per key; scalars replace. Use layer house-style preset_spec onto chain without restating knobs. .style style_template() layer onto cascade. <style_template | NULL>: default NULL. supplied, every layer template accumulated via style() replayed order engine time, per-spec style() layers .spec. Use attach sponsor's reusable house style chain without restating every per-region rule. .reset Discard spec's existing preset applying .... <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE, spec's prior preset_spec () dropped ... knobs merged onto fresh preset_spec() defaults. knobs, per-spec preset cleared back NULL (spec falls set_preset() preset_spec() defaults).
code: pagehead = list(   left   = "Protocol: ABC-123",   center = "Draft",   right  = "Page {page} of {npages}" ) pagehead = list(   left  = c("Protocol: ABC-123", "Analysis Set: Safety"),   right = "Page {page} of {npages}"   # scalar -> body-edge row ) # Landscape A4, 8pt body, slim margins for one wide table. preset(   orientation = "landscape",   paper_size  = "a4",   font_size   = 8,   margins     = c(0.75, 0.5, 0.75, 0.5) )

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining paginate(), style(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Per-spec, chained. preset() per-spec override — verb returns modified spec, composable pipe alongside cols() / headers() / paginate(). Use single table needs one-geometry (e.g. landscape A4 one wide efficacy summary inside portfolio portrait letter tables). Merge, replace. second preset() call merges scalar knobs onto spec's existing preset; unspecified knobs keep prior value. five named-list knobs (alignment / rules / fonts / colors / padding) lower style_layer records preset@style via .preset_args_to_layers() (internal) append call order; layer order precedence within engine cascade, later preset() call's lowered attribute wins earlier one cell. Pass .reset = TRUE discard existing knobs start preset_spec() defaults. preset(.spec, .reset = TRUE) knobs clears per-spec override entirely (spec falls set_preset() preset_spec() defaults render time). Direct preset_spec() calls bypass lowering. five named-list knobs longer slots preset_spec S7 class — exist preset() / set_preset() arguments lower @style. preset_spec(rules = list(...)) (analogous direct calls) raise "unused argument". Wrap calls tabular(...) |> preset(...) lowering helper fires layers land @style. Cascade set_preset(). engine resolves active preset order: (1) spec's per-call preset (verb), (2) session default attached via set_preset(), (3) preset_spec() factory defaults. first non-NULL layer wins; layers field-merged across cascade.

text: .spec tabular_spec apply minimal theme . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed partial matching bind ... knob spec slot. ... Named preset knobs. title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: sets, theme (lowest) precedence explicit later style() wins: Rules. Drops booktabs toprule bottomrule (outer frame), keeping midrule column labels muted column-spanner spanrule. Equivalent preset(rules = list(toprule = "none", bottomrule = "none")). Weight. Sets bold = FALSE title, column-header, subgroup-label, group-header surfaces. HTML backend overrides font-weight: 600 class default inline font-weight: normal; paginated backends (RTF / LaTeX / PDF / DOCX) suppress surface's bold run. Last verb wins. weight layers ride theme tier, later explicit style(bold = TRUE, .= cells_title()) (surface) re-bolds . Treat preset_minimal() theme baseline override individual surfaces afterwards. Markdown. GFM represent colour / background / font surface; rendering styled surface .md emits one-time tabular_warning_fidelity degrades gracefully. Weight (bold) italic carry .

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Renders tabular_spec interactively. default behaviour mirrors gt::gt(): convert spec htmltools tag list let htmltools dispatch — RStudio + Positron viewer panes, Quarto / Rmd notebook inline, Databricks displayHTML, plain-console cat() handled without IDE- specific branching.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: x tabular_spec render. <tabular_spec>: required. object hand emit(). ... Forwarded htmltools::print / .tags(). Use pass id, style, class overrides wrapping <div>. view Open viewer? <logical(1)>: default interactive()“. role gt::gt's `view` argument: passes htmltools `browse = view`. Set `view = FALSE` suppress viewer one call (e.g. capture HTML string without launching window). output Force specific preview format. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL (auto). See output argument section full list. session default can set via options(tabular_print_output = "cli") users prefer structural summary HTML preview.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Invisibly returns x. Side effect: opens viewer, inlines chunk, cat()s output.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Dispatch. print() delegates .tags.tabular_spec() returns htmltools::tagList. tag list handed htmltools's print method browse = view: htmltools opens IDE viewer one registered, inlines Quarto / Rmd chunk running inside one, cat()s HTML neither applies. is_rstudio() / is_positron() / is_notebook() heuristics — htmltools already knows. view argument. Defaults interactive(), universal -switch gt::gt() uses. Non-interactive contexts (Rscript, R CMD check, CI, devtools::test) bypass viewer automatically. Pass view = FALSE explicitly interactive prompt suppress viewer single call. output argument. Forces specific preview format instead default HTML-via-htmltools path. One : "html" — default, explicit. "md" / "markdown" — cat() markdown source console (round-trips backend_md). "latex" — cat() markdown source temporary placeholder (real LaTeX preview lands backend_latex). "rtf" / "docx" / "pdf" — render HTML preview emit cli note pointing emit() real artefact. viewer pane render RTF / OOXML, deliberately compile tinytex every autoprint. "cli" — print structural cli-tree summary (props, headers, sort, pagination, preset). Useful debugging spec composition without paying HTML render cost. Robustness. HTML render wrapped tryCatch; rendering fails reason printer falls back cli-tree summary cli::cli_warn() describing failure, broken spec never crashes REPL. Tempdir. Preview HTML files live getOption("tabular_preview_dir", default = tempdir()). Override option keep stable location (handy Linux distros browsers read access /tmp/).

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: Stash preset_spec package-internal session environment. Every subsequent tabular() chain attach preset() inherits knobs render time. Mirrors ggplot2's ggplot2::theme_set(): one call front, many tables downstream.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
code: set_preset(new = NULL, ..., .template = NULL, .style = NULL, .reset = FALSE)

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: new preset_spec install wholesale. <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. non-NULL, replaces session preset one call without touching knobs. primary use save/restore round-trip (old <- set_preset(...); set_preset(old)) — new accepts preset_spec previously returned set_preset() get_preset(). Mutually exclusive ..., .template, .style, .reset: passing alongside non-NULL new raises tabular_error_input. ... Named preset knobs. shape preset(); see verb full list recognised knobs. Unknown names raise tabular_error_input. Mutually exclusive non-NULL new. .template preset_spec bulk-apply .... <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. semantics preset()'s .template: every knob set away factory default feeds base layer; user-supplied ... knobs merge top shallow-merge per list-valued knob. .style style_template() layer session default. <style_template | NULL>: default NULL. semantics preset()'s .style: template's accumulated layers feed session-default style, layered per-spec style() calls. .reset Discard existing session preset applying .... <logical(1)>: default FALSE. knobs, clears session default back NULL.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: previous session preset_spec (invisible). Returns NULL session preset attached prior call. Capture round-trip temporary override: old <- set_preset(...); set_preset(old). Mirrors ggplot2::theme_set() base::options() — canonical tidyverse save/restore primitive.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: Persistence. session preset lives package-internal environment populated tabular loaded emptied namespace unloads. -disk persistence; set default top analysis script (project-level .Rprofile) sticky house style needed. Merge, replace. second set_preset() call merges knobs onto existing session preset; unspecified knobs keep prior value. Pass .reset = TRUE discard existing session preset start preset_spec() defaults. set_preset(.reset = TRUE) knobs clears session default back NULL. Save restore. Every call returns previous session preset invisibly, primitive ggplot2's ggplot2::theme_set() ships. Capture , render, restore passing saved value back positional new argument:   prior NULL (session preset ever attached), restore set_preset(.reset = TRUE) instead — set_preset(NULL) shape set_preset() falls factory defaults rather clearing session. Cascade preset(). per-spec preset() always wins session default. session default fills spec carries preset .

the # second half produces efficacy tables at 10pt on landscape A4. A # single `set_preset(.reset = TRUE, ...)` resets the cascade before # the second batch starts. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") get_preset()@font_size  # 9 #> [1] 9  set_preset(   .reset      = TRUE,   font_size   = 10,   orientation = "landscape",   paper_size  = "a4" ) get_preset()@orientation  # "landscape" #> [1] "landscape"  # Reset the session default so subsequent examples / R sessions # are not affected. set_preset(.reset = TRUE)  # ---- Example 3: Save and restore around a renegade table ---- # # Most of the submission renders portrait letter at 9pt. One # renegade efficacy table needs landscape A4 at 10pt. Capture # the prior session preset, render the renegade, then restore. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter")  old <- set_preset(   font_size   = 10,   paper_size  = "a4",   orientation = "landscape" ) # ... one renegade render ... if (is.null(old)) {   set_preset(.reset = TRUE)   # was no prior — clear } else {   set_preset(old)              # round-trip via the positional `new` arg } get_preset()@paper_size  # "letter" — restored #> [1] "letter"  # ---- Example 4: Snapshot current preset, mutate, restore ---- # # Capture whatever the session preset is right now (may be NULL), # let a downstream helper mutate it, then put it back when done. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") snapshot <- get_preset()  # Simulate downstream code mutating session state. set_preset(font_size = 11, orientation = "landscape")  # Restore. The wholesale-install path of `set_preset(new)` # accepts any `preset_spec` returned by `get_preset()` / # `set_preset()`. if (is.null(snapshot)) {   set_preset(.reset = TRUE) } else {   set_preset(snapshot) } get_preset()@font_size    # 9 — restored #> [1] 9  # Reset for subsequent examples / R sessions. set_preset(.reset = TRUE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Attach sort_spec tabular_spec. engine applies sort pagination, may reference column spec@data whether column declared cols().

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
code: sort_rows(.spec, by = character(), descending = FALSE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: .spec tabular_spec attach sort . <tabular_spec>: required. Ordered column names sort , precedence order. <character>: default character(). Length 0 accepted (-op sort). May reference columns declared cols() — sort-helper columns ride along engine. Restriction: Every entry must column spec@data. reference arm columns produced pivot_across(); pivot upstream sort instead. Arm cells hold rendered stat strings (e.g. "75.2 (8.3)") order meaningfully.   descending Per-key sort direction. <logical(1) | logical(length())>: default FALSE. TRUE sorts corresponding key descending; length 1 recycles every key. Restriction: NAs. Length must 1 length(). Tip: mixed-direction multi-key sorts, pass length() values; engine inverts xtfrm rank descending key calls order() keys.
code: # Two-key hierarchical sort: SOC clusters by descending count, # each PT nested under its SOC. sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining style(), paginate(), preset(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Replace, stack. second sort_rows() call REPLACES prior sort — sort single spec, stackable list. Call arguments clear. NA last, regardless direction. NA values sort key placed end whether key ascending descending (matching order(..., na.last = TRUE)). Factor levels drive order. Factor columns sort factor levels, character label. CDISC BOR ordering (CR < PR < SD < NON-CR/NON-PD < PD < NE < MISSING) survives tabular pipeline without explicit mutate() — coerce stat_label factor levels clinical order upstream, sort_rows(= "stat_label") rest.

what: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: One verb, one cascade. style() call appends single style_layer (location + style attributes) spec template. Layers accumulate declaration order; engine merges render time later layers win per attribute, NA-valued fields leave prior layer intact.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
code: style(.spec, ..., .at = cells_body())

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: .spec tabular_spec tabular_style_template. <tabular_spec | tabular_style_template>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short attribute name ... spec slot. piping style_template() |> style(...) layers accumulate onto template instead spec. ... Named style attributes. least one required. See Style attributes section recognised names. .Location object selecting surface layer targets. <tabular_location>: default cells_body(). Build one cells_*() constructors; see cells_body() siblings. Dot-prefixed (tidyverse convention) comes ... — way user-passed style attribute can never collide arg's name.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: updated tabular_spec (tabular_style_template, called one).

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: .argument selects surface layer targets. Every region rendered page cells_*() constructor: cells_body()             — body cells (default) cells_headers()          — column header band cells_group_headers()    — synthetic group-header rows cells_title()            — title block cells_subgroup_labels()  — subgroup banner row cells_footnotes()        — footnote block cells_pagehead()         — page-header band cells_pagefoot()         — page-footer band cells_table()            — table-wide regions (outer borders, body-row separators) Body filters live cells_body(): = 1:3 integer-index rows, j = "Total" column-name targeting, = <expr> quosure-captured predicate evaluated spec@data. figure() spec shares chrome surfaces, style() accepts figure cells_title(), cells_footnotes(), cells_pagehead(), cells_pagefoot() ; figure body, column headers, subgroup banner, locations raise error.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: layer carries style_node built .... Recognised attribute names: Text — bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_family, font_size Alignment — halign ("left" / "center" / "right"), valign ("top" / "middle" / "bottom") Borders — border (umbrella), border_top, border_bottom, border_left, border_right (takes brdr() value literal "none"); per-side scalars border_<side>_{style,width,color} finer control Padding — padding (scalar applies four sides; named vector c(top = , right = , bottom = , left = ) sets side); per-side scalars padding_<side> directly Spacing — blank_above, blank_below (integer blank lines / block — cells_title() / cells_footnotes() / cells_subgroup_labels()) Inline — pretext, posttext (literal text prepended / appended around cell value) Unknown attribute names emit cli::cli_warn drop constructed node; engine never sees foreign property.

what: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: Build reusable, composable style template chaining style() calls tabular_style_template. template carries ordered list style_layer records can attached preset() / set_preset() .style argument — every downstream tabular() chain inherits template's layers via engine cascade.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: style_template()  is_style_template(x)

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: x R object. predicate inspects class via inherits(); introspection performed.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: tabular_style_template — small S3 list layers slot. Pipe style() add layers.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: One verb, two surfaces. style(.spec_or_template, ..., .= ...) call attaches layer per-table spec also accumulates layers onto template. Symmetric API — need learn second function multi-table use case. Submission workflow. submission typically renders 100–200 tables one visual identity. Build template top submission script, pass set_preset(.style = template), every subsequent tabular() produces output inherits column-header rules, group-header bolding, title spacing, outer-frame borders without single per-table style() call. Cascade order. Engines apply layers low--high priority: backend defaults → session preset's @style → spec preset's @style → per-spec style() layers. Later layers override prior ones per attribute; NA fields leave prior layer's value place.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: # ---- Sponsor "house style" composed once ---- # # The result becomes the default look for every table rendered # against this preset. No per-table style() boilerplate. house <- style_template() |>   style(background = "#DBE4F0", .at = cells_headers(level = -1)) |>   style(color = "#1F3B5C", .at = cells_group_headers()) |>   style(     border_top    = brdr("thick", "double"),     border_bottom = brdr("thick", "double"),     .at = cells_headers()   ) |>   style(blank_above = 1, blank_below = 1, .at = cells_title())  length(house$layers) #> [1] 4  # ---- Verify class ---- is_style_template(house) #> [1] TRUE

what: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Attach subgroup_spec tabular_spec. render time engine partitions spec@data unique values , runs full resolve pipeline per group, concatenates results. hard page break inserted groups — every subgroup value starts page. centred banner line appears column-header rule every page group (including continuation pages), matching canonical submission page-layout convention.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
code: subgroup(   .spec,   by,   label = NULL,   big_n = NULL,   big_n_fmt = "\n(N={n})",   keep_empty = FALSE )

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: .spec tabular_spec partition. <tabular_spec>: required. Column name(s) partition . <character>: required. Must reference column spec@data. Length-0 (character(0)) clears partition. Matches = arg convention sort_rows(). Multi-variable. Pass c("var1", "var2") cross every combination present data. Multi-var partitions require explicit label template (single-var auto-default generalise). label Banner template. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. Glue-style template {column_name} placeholders. NULL derives default partition variable's attr(data[[]], "label") (falling back column name). Tip: reference auxiliary columns inline BigN qualifier constant within group — e.g. "Cohort: {cohort} (N = {n})". Restriction: Every {col} reference must column spec@data. Unknown columns raise tabular_error_subgroup_template_unknown_col. big_n Per-page BigN denominators. <data.frame> | NULL: default NULL. table giving (N=x) denominator arm's header show subgroup page. arm named appears header — either data column (N rides column's leaf label) headers() band label (N rides spanner band). Ns non-negative whole numbers; provide one per combination present data. Accepts either shape: Wide — column(s) plus one numeric column per arm (cells Ns). Long — column(s) plus one arm-name column one numeric N column, .e. dplyr::count() / summarise() output used directly reshaping.   Requirement: band keying needs headers() subgroup() pipeline; arm name must resolve exactly one leaf XOR one band. Every missing per-page N call-time error, never silently wrong denominator. Note: per-arm N renders every backend. paged backends (RTF, PDF / LaTeX, DOCX) carry column header repeats every page subgroup. HTML Markdown continuous (one stacked table, one header), instead emit per-arm N row directly subgroup banner, (N=x) aligned beneath arm column. big_n_fmt Per-page BigN template. <character(1)>: default "\n(N={n})". Appended arm's header label, {n} substituted page/column's integer N. {n} token allowed; default puts N line arm name. keep_empty Retain zero-N crossings. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. FALSE (default), crossing combination data rows dropped. TRUE, retained rendered empty-state page carrying banner, report shows every combination explicitly. multi-variable crossings can produce empty combinations; single-variable partition never . Note: empty group data row read constant-within-group banner values , label template references non-column (e.g. auxiliary BigN column) resolves token NA empty group; -column tokens always resolve.
code: # Wide: one column per arm. wide <- tibble::tribble(   ~sex, ~placebo, ~drug_50, ~Total,   "F",       24L,       9L,    42L,   "M",       18L,      15L,    47L ) # Long: count()-style, pivoted internally. Equivalent to `wide`. long <- tibble::tribble(   ~sex, ~arm,      ~n,   "F",  "placebo", 24L,   "F",  "drug_50",  9L,   "F",  "Total",   42L,   "M",  "placebo", 18L,   "M",  "drug_50", 15L,   "M",  "Total",   47L ) spec |> subgroup(by = "sex", big_n = long)

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining resolve via as_grid() / emit().

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Label glue-style template. label carries {col} placeholders, engine substitutes placeholder FIRST ROW group's filtered data — column whose value constant within group (BigN, cohort descriptor, qualifier text) can ride banner. Columns vary within group also resolve, always first row's value; pre-compute aggregates upstream. Default label (label = NULL, single var): engine generates "<attr(data[[]], 'label') %||% >: {<>}", subgroup(= "cohort") renders banners like "Cohort: " "Cohort: B" without configuration. Replace, stack. second subgroup() call REPLACES prior partition — subgroup single spec, stackable list. Passing = character(0) clears slot, though typical clinical pipelines set partition front. Display-side . subgroup() partitions pre-summarised wide data frame; aggregate, filter, weight. user supplies one summary row per displayed row per group; tabular's job solely lay per-group banner page break. Multi-variable crossing. = c("SEX", "AGEGR1") partitions every combination present data (first variable varies slowest, matching expand.grid() convention). explicit label template required multi-var partitions since single-var default "<var>: {<var>}" generalise; raise tabular_error_subgroup_label_required otherwise. Auto-hide partition + template columns. Every column named , plus every column referenced via {col} placeholder label, automatically flips visible = FALSE engine time. Users restate col_spec(visible = FALSE) inside cols() columns — mirroring col_spec(indent = ...) auto-hide ergonomic.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Table 14.2.1 Vital Signs Visit Safety Population   Descriptive statistics treatment arm.   Vital Signs Visit     Vital Signs Sex Visit     Vital Signs Visit     Pooled (sex partition)
code: # ---- Example 1: Vital signs split into one page set per sex ---- # # The simplest partition: a single clinical variable. Each `sex` # value gets its own page set with a centred `Sex: <value>` banner # above the column-header rule on every page, separated by hard page # breaks. With no `label` template the banner uses the variable's # `label` attribute when present (set here), falling back to the # column name. what: tabular: Render Tables, Listings, and Figures for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
title: tabular: Render Tables, Listings, and Figures for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
text: Render clinical submission tables, listings, figures 'RTF', 'LaTeX', 'HTML', 'PDF', 'DOCX' pre-summarised data frames, external 'Java' 'SAS' dependency. Features include decimal alignment via font metrics, multi-level column headers passthrough leaves, predicate-targeted cell styling, footnotes, group-aware pagination, figures wrap plot image page chrome table. Built Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Analysis Data Model (ADaM) workflows regulatory submissions agencies Food Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA), Pharmaceuticals Medical Devices Agency (PMDA).

title: tabular: Render Tables, Listings, and Figures for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
text: Maintainer: Vignesh Thanikachalam .vignesh@gmail.com

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Wrap pre-summarised data frame tabular_spec ready verb chain. tabular() entry verb — owns data, titles, footnotes slots; every downstream verb (cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset()) returns updated spec chaining, terminating emit() (write file) as_grid() (resolve without writing).

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
code: tabular(data, titles = NULL, footnotes = NULL, empty_text = NULL)

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: data display rows. <data.frame>: required. Pre-summarised wide-format data; tibbles, data.tables, arrow tables coerced via .data.frame(). Factor columns preserved (levels drive sort_rows()). Restriction: least one column; column names must unique. Zero rows accepted (engine renders "data" stub). Interaction: cards-format counterparts (cdisc_saf_demo_ard, cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard) accepted directly; pipe pivot_across() first. titles Page-title block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. element renders centred line; embedded \n wraps within row. backend collapses unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title. Restriction: NAs. element supports glue-style {expr} interpolation: braces evaluated R code calling environment build time, e.g. "N total = {sum(n)}". Double brace ({{ }}) literal one. md() / html() element passed without interpolation.   footnotes Page-footnote block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. User-supplied prose rows ; backend appends program-path / program-name / timestamp band render time. Restriction: NAs. element supports glue-style {expr} interpolation (see titles).   empty_text Placeholder shown data zero rows. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. display resolves data rows, backends still emit full page chrome — column structure present — column headers, place message body rows sit. NULL (default) inherits house-style wording preset(empty_text = ...) set, falling back built-"data available report". Pass sponsor study wording (localized string, "subjects met criteria table.", protocol-qualified line) override per table; glue {expr} interpolation md() / html() honoured, exactly like title line. message renders single horizontally centred row table body, first data row otherwise sit.
code: # Canonical 3-line title block with BigN-qualified population. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) titles = c(   "Table 14.3.1",   "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",   "Safety Population" ) # Canonical 3-line footnote block. footnotes = c(   "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.",   "Percentages based on N per treatment group.",   "TEAE = treatment-emergent adverse event." )

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: tabular_spec S7 object. Pipe cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset() build display, emit() render as_grid() resolve without writing.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Pre-summarised input contract. data one row per displayed row final table. tabular() aggregate, filter, weight, generate subtotal rows — happen upstream cards, dplyr, SAS. upstream long cards::ard_stack() ARD, pipe pivot_across() first land wide shape tabular() accepts. Multi-line titles footnotes contract. Clinical tables routinely carry 2-4 title rows 1-4 user footnote rows. Pass row one element character vector; backend renders element line, collapsing unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular

title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: S7 class definitions backing tabular's display-side IR. Users construct directly except col_spec(); every class built chained verb pipeline (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset() -> as_grid() / emit()).

title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: class set intentionally small (~11 concepts) IR fits one mental model: Every spec slot typed: verb mutate slot invalid value fails construction time (S7 validator runs last-line defense behind cli-friendly verb-level validators). Class predicates. class matching is_<name>() predicate; see tabular_predicates full list.

title: tabular 0.2.0
text: documentation site gained AI / Agents section linking tabular skill (LLM coding agents), auto-generated llms.txt, concatenated llms-full.txt, mirroring LLM-friendly documentation pattern. col_spec(group_display = "header_row") now collapses single-member group one flush-left row (group value becomes row label, still carrying cells_group_headers() styling) instead emitting redundant header plus lone indented child, longer emits empty header blank NA group value. Multi-member groups unchanged. figure() renders figure (“F” TFL) every backend (RTF, LaTeX, PDF, HTML, DOCX, Markdown), wrapping ggplot, recorded base-R plot, zero-argument drawing function, PNG / JPG file submission chrome table (titles, footnotes, page header footer). image placed body content-box halign valign (default centred), exact paged backends approximate continuous ones. list input emits one figure per page, optionally driven meta data frame whose columns become per-page {token} values. continuous backends (HTML Markdown) multi-page figure’s shared titles footnotes render stacked plots; supplying meta switches per-page chrome. is_figure_spec() tests new spec, emit() writes file. figure’s titles, footnotes, page header / footer honour style() preset() cosmetic knobs (fonts, colours, alignment, padding) preset(spacing = ...) inter-section gaps, just like table; styling (absent) body, headers, subgroup error. drawing function raises error aborts clear message naming failing page. new package dependency: plots rasterise base grDevices ggplot2 (Suggests) ggplot passed. pivot_across() gained aux argument bind auxiliary comparison columns (difference, hazard ratio, p-value) second ARD, aligned 1:1 row_group key appended trailing columns. pivot_across()’s column argument now accepts reserved tokens .variable .stat make analysis variable column band: c(".variable", "<arm>") lays variables side side statistics rows, c(".variable", ".stat") spreads statistic column arm row stub. Per-variable statistic / decimals resolve inside band. pivot_across()’s decimals may now list keyed row_group values, per-group precision one call. preset(font_family = ...) generic chains ("mono" / "sans" / "serif") now lead ubiquitous Microsoft Office face (Courier New / Arial / Times New Roman) keep metric-compatible Liberation face last fallback, Word shows font reader actually installed Windows / macOS instead phantom “Liberation Mono” font menu. faces metric-compatible, layout, line breaks, decimal alignment unchanged. bare font_family = "Courier New" now also leads Courier New (previously resolved led Liberation Mono). package bundles fonts; arbitrary named face ("IBM Plex Mono", "Source Code Pro", etc.) emitted verbatim consuming application resolve.

title: tabular 0.2.0
text: col_spec() gained single indent argument (integer fixed level every body row, column name per-row depth); previous indent_by argument usage = "indent" value removed favour . explicit indent group_display = "header_row" host now suppresses section auto-indent, yielding single rather double indent. paginate() removed -op panels = "auto"; panels now positive integer. pivot_across() longer pre-indents stat_label two leading spaces; indentation applied downstream renderer via col_spec(usage = "group") / group_display, header_row stub longer double-indents.

title: tabular 0.2.0
text: Every warning now carries tabular_warning_<kind> class (input, runtime, layout, fidelity) mirroring tabular_error_<kind> error taxonomy, warnings can caught selectively; former tabular_warn_layout class renamed tabular_warning_layout. col_spec() now warns group_display group_skip set non-group column (knobs inert unless usage = "group"). cols() / cols_apply() now restore column’s visibility reset group_display later call, merge every column attribute field-completely (previously default value merged back fields dropped). emit() now centres decimal-aligned columns every backend instead right-aligning uniformly NBSP-padded block, values sit centred column header (cross-backend parity). emit() DOCX now declares -class <w:altName> fallback font, reader missing primary face substitutes metric-compatible face class (mono stays mono) instead panose-guessing serif. brings DOCX line RTF (\*\falt) PDF / LaTeX (\IfFontExistsTF cascade) backends, already declared -class fallback chain. emit() HTML now applies per-cell body alignment specificity-bumped .tabular-table td.text-* rule, decimal, centred right-aligned columns actually render aligned; previously base .tabular-table td { text-align: left } rule outranked plain alignment class every body cell silently fell back left. emit() HTML now aligns running page header footer table width via centred fit-content container, instead spanning full document width. emit() HTML Markdown longer repeats subgroup() banner mid-table group taller one estimated page. continuous backends draw one banner per subgroup value; print-page-break markers within group unchanged, paged backends (RTF, LaTeX, DOCX) still repeat banner every continuation page design. emit() PDF / LaTeX longer renders table wider printable area: tabularray adds per-column separation outside column’s wd, column widths now reduced separation rendered table total matches resolved width (RTF / DOCX cell widths) instead bleeding right margin. (#27) emit() PDF / LaTeX now renders column headers bold, matching DOCX, RTF HTML backends. emit() PDF / LaTeX now sets body font size \begin{document} (survives) re-asserts inside running header / footer, 8pt table longer renders body page chrome 10pt document-class default. (#27) emit() PDF / LaTeX now tightens gap running page header title (body running footer) one line via \headsep \footskip, instead wide document-class default. emit() PDF / LaTeX longer overflows figure onto second page: image placed flexible \vfill glue rather fixed-height box reconstructed just page height, multi-page figure (example one Kaplan-Meier plot per treatment arm) now fits one page per plot. emit() RTF DOCX longer emits phantom blank page figure plot. figure box reserves line closing paragraph every paged backend appends exact-height image, RTF exits table context \pard\par next section break, DOCX starts continuation page structural <w:pageBreakBefore/> instead standalone page-break paragraph stranded blank page. multi-page figure driven per-page meta also sizes page’s image box page’s interpolated footnotes, longer-footnote page longer overflows. emit() aborts clear tabular_error_layout message figure’s titles footnotes alone exceed printable height, instead failing opaque graphics-device error. emit() RTF now leads body font slot first face explicit font_family stack instead Linux-first default chain, classes mono stack \fmodern (fixed pitch) way DOCX classes modern. font_family = c("Courier New", "Liberation Mono", "Courier") request now renders Courier New mono Word instead substituting serif, named primary face leads font table rather demoted \*\falt alternate. emit() RTF now emits section break panels (n-1 breaks n panels) rather every panel, single-panel table longer ends trailing section break Word renders phantom blank page. emit() RTF now reserves one line per running-header row \headery, multi-row page header (example protocol row plus analysis-set row) longer bleeds back body tips last table rows, table’s trailing paragraph, onto phantom second page. emit() now renders zero-row empty-state page normal table whose body single full-span, horizontally centred “data” message row, first data row sit: table chrome (titles, column header) leads, message follows body, footnote trails immediately, flowing compactly top page blank space . message centred backend’s native cell alignment every backend (RTF, LaTeX, PDF, HTML, DOCX, Markdown); longer vertically centred relocated page margins. natural-height message row plus trailing footnote overflow, recurring phantom-page bug stays fixed. subgroup(keep_empty = TRUE) empty crossing renders way, one message row panel. emit() now closes zero-row empty-state table’s data region body bottom rule every backend, “data” page carries closing rule populated table . rule follows rules preset (custom bottomrule width / style / colour honoured, bottomrule = "none" drops ) instead absent (RTF / DOCX) fixed default. emit() PDF / LaTeX now wraps table footnote table width rather overrunning : footnote minipage longer double-counts column separation column widths already fold , narrow table footnote text stays within table-width footnote rule instead spilling past (past printable width). figure() paged-table pagination now size body box number wrapped chrome lines long title footnote actually occupies printable width, element count, wrapped footnote longer pushes content onto second page DOCX (paged backend). Wrapping computed greedy word packing font metrics. (#26) paginate() now collapses zero-row table single empty-state page; horizontal panels longer multiply empty table several identical blank pages. preset()’s decimal_metrics knob gained "afm" (now default), making decimal alignment width-exact proportional fonts via bundled font metrics; Markdown output keeps character padding. preset() gained empty_text knob, house-style default zero-row message per-table tabular(empty_text = ...) still overrides. preset(spacing = ...) now drives blank lines around subgroup banner (subgroup region) around figure() title footnote; Markdown table title footnote gaps now follow knob. preset(width_mode = "window") now sizes auto columns proportionally content width fill page, instead giving every column equal share. subgroup() gained keep_empty argument; keep_empty = TRUE zero-N crossing retained rendered empty-state page carrying banner instead dropped. subgroup() longer errors zero-row input; table renders empty-state placeholder subgroup banner. tabular() gained empty_text, message shown table zero data rows (default “data available report”). Zero-row tables now render full page chrome column headers message placed body, replacing previous bare “(rows)” marker.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-11 First release. tabular renders pre-summarised clinical tables listings RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF, DOCX one immutable verb pipeline, external Java SAS dependency. initial CRAN version consolidates entire pre-release development cycle: every feature bug fix designed, implemented, tested first release. Scope. release covers tables listings. Figure (graph) output yet supported focus next release.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: tabular() takes pre-summarised wide data frame (one input row one display row) plus multi-line titles footnotes. cols() / col_spec() set per-column usage, label, format, width, alignment (including decimal alignment via bundled font metrics), visibility, NA text, per-row indent (indent_by), group display. cols() takes .default fallback col_spec(), cols_apply() applies one col_spec() many columns (name predicate), resolving {.name} token label matched column. headers() builds multi-level column-header bands passthrough leaves; sort_rows() sorts hidden numeric keys; subgroup() partitions {col} banner template optional per-page big_n. style() applies predicate-targeted cell styling cells_*() location helpers; preset() / set_preset() / get_preset() set cosmetic defaults (fonts, colours, rules, padding, alignment, page chrome) per table per session, reusable house styles via style_template(). footnote() attaches auto-numbered footnotes (letters, numbers, symbols) cell, column header, title, deduped id byte-identical across backends. paginate() group-aware pagination auto-computed per-page row budget; emit() writes chosen backend (creating parent directories create_dir), as_grid() resolves grid without /O.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: Native emission RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF (via LaTeX), DOCX single resolved grid; output verified per-backend byte snapshots cross-backend CDISC-pilot qualification (inst/qualification/). Inline markup via md() html(). Glue-style {expr} interpolation static labels, titles, footnotes, header band labels, evaluated calling environment build time. Significant-whitespace preservation across backends (preset(whitespace = "preserve"), default). check_latex() reports LaTeX-package availability PDF output prints exact tlmgr_install() remedy anything missing — including CTAN-mirror pin (tinytex::tlmgr_repo("auto")) install stalls behind redirecting default mirror (commonly Windows); check_fonts() fonts, per backend.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: Bundled synthetic CDISC-pilot demo data (cdisc_saf_demo, cdisc_saf_ae, cdisc_saf_aesocpt, cdisc_saf_vital, cdisc_eff_resp, BigN frames cdisc_saf_n / cdisc_eff_n, long-format ARD companions cdisc_saf_demo_ard / cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard) examples vignettes.
